c:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\README_FOR_CLAUDE_PROPERTYOPS.md
i have given this task "A CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations system with main features including offline user accounts, fitness goal management, study/review planning, inbound receiving workflows, master data management, and a unified notification center. The Vue.js web interface serves three roles: Regular User (students/trainees), Warehouse/Operations Staff, and Administrator. Regular Users sign up and sign in with a local username and password, then complete a fitness assessment (height in ft/in, weight in lb, optional body measurements) and set goals such as “Lose 12 lb in 12 weeks” or “Improve flexibility over 8 weeks,” with phase milestones displayed on a dashboard and progress feedback shown as weekly check-ins. They also create study and memorization plans tied to locally managed academic structures (term, school, major, class, course), track daily completion, and tag “forgetting points” after reviews; the UI provides reminders, streak indicators, and a one-click export to a file for cross-account migration. Operations Staff use a receiving workspace for purchase, transfer, and return receipts: they scan or enter an inbound reference, record quantities, run a pass/fail quality inspection with notes, accept system putaway recommendations, and resolve discrepancies through guided prompts before completion. Administrators manage academic master data with bulk Excel/CSV import/export, review validation errors inline, merge duplicates, and view change history. A messaging center within the UI consolidates announcements, reminders, and follow-ups with read receipts and delivery status visible to authorized users; delivery is in-app only in offline mode, while email/SMS/WeCom are configurable but disabled unless explicitly enabled in a connected environment.

The backend uses Spring Boot to implement REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js client, enforcing a decoupled architecture where all actions are validated server-side and persisted in MySQL for offline-first local deployment. Authentication uses salted password hashing (bcrypt) with lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes and session timeout after 30 minutes of inactivity; role-based access control limits administrative actions, inbound posting/unposting, moderation, and report handling to authorized roles. Inbound workflow processing is state-machine-driven (Draft → Receiving → Inspection → Putaway → Completed/Rejected) with automatic posting only when required fields are satisfied; discrepancy rules include quantity variance thresholds (e.g., >2% or >5 units requires supervisor review) and mandatory reason codes. Fitness goals apply dynamic adjustment rules such as recalculating weekly targets if two consecutive check-ins are missed, while preserving an audit trail. Master data versioning stores effective dates (MM/DD/YYYY) and blocks deletion when referenced by plans; merges preserve the surviving record with a complete change audit. High-performance querying is supported through MySQL partitioning by term and month, pre-aggregated tables for dashboards, result caching with a 5-minute TTL, pagination defaults of 25 rows per page, async loading for heavy reports, and built-in slow-query logging with an admin performance screen. All personal metrics are encrypted at rest using AES-256, sensitive fields are masked in logs and exports by default, and exports require a password-protected file with a configurable retention policy (default 30 days) plus full account deletion that hard-deletes identifiers while retaining de-identified audit events for compliance."
so to build this task please follow the protoype readme file

# PropertyOps Maintenance & Compliance Backend — Build Instructions for Claude

## How to use this file

Attach this README together with the original business/task prompt.

- The **original task prompt** remains the source of truth for business requirements.
- This README is the **execution guide** that tells you how to implement the project so it survives a **static-only delivery audit**.
- Build this as a **real, production-shaped backend**, not a demo, not a partial prototype, and not a loose code sample.

---

# 1. Primary delivery objective

Build a **Docker-first, offline-first, single-node Go backend** for multifamily property operations using:

- **Go**
- **Gin**
- **GORM**
- **MySQL**
- **Docker / Docker Compose**

The system must manage, in one local deployment:

- authentication and sessions
- tenants and property/unit context
- maintenance work orders
- internal notifications and message threads
- governance reports and enforcement
- offline payment intents and settlement postings
- reconciliation and discrepancy handling
- analytics and scheduled local reports
- health checks, logs, backups, restore validation, and auditability

The system must work **fully offline on a local network**, with **no internet dependency and no third-party runtime service dependency**.

---

# 2. Build for the static reviewer, not just for runtime

The evaluator will perform a **static-only audit**. They will:

- not run the project
- not start Docker
- not run tests automatically
- not modify code
- judge the repository against the business prompt and the acceptance criteria

Therefore, for every important requirement, make sure there is visible, traceable evidence in the repository.

## For every core requirement, provide all five forms of evidence

1. **Schema / migrations**
2. **Typed DTOs / validation**
3. **Service / business logic**
4. **Route registration / handlers**
5. **Tests + documentation**

If one of these is missing for a core feature, the static reviewer is likely to mark it partial or failed.

---

# 3. Non-negotiable implementation rules

## 3.1 Backend only

- No frontend.
- Do not add placeholder UI.
- Do not add unrelated web pages or mock dashboards.

## 3.2 Offline only

Do not use or depend on:

- outbound HTTP calls
- email providers
- SMS providers
- push providers
- OAuth providers
- cloud storage
- Redis
- Kafka
- external payment processors
- third-party callbacks
- hosted schedulers
- webhook integrations that require the internet

All behavior must remain local.

## 3.3 Docker is first-class, not optional

The solution must be designed around a **single-node Docker Compose deployment**.

Include real, statically consistent:

- `deploy/Dockerfile`
- `deploy/docker-compose.yml`
- `.dockerignore`
- `.env.example`
- `README.md` instructions
- migration container flow
- healthchecks
- mounted persistent local volumes

## 3.4 Use real persistence

- MySQL via GORM
- SQL migrations must exist
- Do not rely only on `AutoMigrate`
- local filesystem storage for attachments, generated reports, logs, backups, and key material

## 3.5 Do not leave core requirements as TODOs

Do not leave the following as stubs, TODOs, or vague documentation-only promises:

- work order lifecycle and dispatch
- attachment validation
- governance review queue and enforcement
- payment expiration and dual approval
- reconciliation
- analytics aggregation
- saved reports and CSV export
- backups and restore validation
- key rotation support
- route-level and object-level authorization

## 3.6 Security must be explicit

Implement and make statically obvious:

- password hashing with bcrypt cost >= 12 or Argon2id
- DB-backed opaque sessions
- idle expiry: 30 minutes
- max session lifetime: 7 days
- AES-256 encryption for sensitive fields at rest
- key versioning and rotation support every 180 days
- route-level authorization
- object-level authorization
- PII masking by default
- explicit-purpose export flow for sensitive data
- admin/internal/debug protection

## 3.7 Full auditability

Every state-changing action must be auditable.

- global audit log for all important changes
- append-only work order events for work-order-specific actions

---

# 4. Core build strategy

## 4.1 Optimize for traceability

A reviewer must be able to trace a requirement through:

- docs
- config
- migration
- model
- DTO validation
- service logic
- route registration
- tests

## 4.2 Prefer clear module boundaries

- thin handlers
- business rules in services
- repositories for persistence access
- reusable validators
- centralized route registration
- dedicated authorization helpers and middleware

## 4.3 Avoid architecture that creates audit noise

Do not introduce:

- unnecessary microservices
- unnecessary sidecars
- unnecessary queues
- unrelated packages or boilerplate
- giant single-file monoliths

---

# 5. Required repository structure

Use a production-shaped structure similar to this:

```text
.
├── README.md
├── Makefile
├── .env.example
├── .dockerignore
├── go.mod
├── cmd/
│   ├── api/

252	# 6. Phase-by-phase implementation plan
253	
254	## Phase 0 — Freeze the contract before writing code
255	
256	Create the docs below first:
257	
258	- `docs/requirement-matrix.md`
259	- `docs/assumptions.md`
260	- `docs/authorization-matrix.md`
261	- `docs/verification-report.md` (start early and keep updating)
262	
263	## 6.0.1 Requirement matrix
264	
265	Create one row per explicit business requirement with these columns:
266	
267	- requirement
268	- schema / migration files
269	- DTO / validation files
270	- service / business logic files
271	- handler / route files
272	- test files
273	- docs
274	- status
275	
276	## 6.0.2 Assumptions to document explicitly
277	
278	Document the following implementation assumptions to avoid reviewer confusion:
279	
280	1. **Issue classification for work orders**
281	   - add `issue_type` and `tags` to support analytics and dispatch
282	2. **Dispatch scope**
283	   - round-robin is deterministic by `property + skill_tag`
284	3. **Business day rule**
285	   - Low priority SLA = Monday-Friday only unless future holiday support is configured
286	4. **Negative rating rate**
287	   - define as ratings `1` or `2`
288	5. **Preferred access window**
289	   - represent as `date`, `start_time`, and `end_time`
290	6. **Message threads**
291	   - implement under Notifications so Governance can report them
292	7. **Local anomaly ingestion**
293	   - protect by local-network allowlist rather than external credentials
294	
295	---
296	
297	## Phase 1 — Docker-first runtime architecture
298	
299	Design the runtime shape before writing business code.
300	
301	## 6.1.1 Required services in Docker Compose
302	
303	Use a single-node layout with:
304	
305	1. `db` — MySQL
306	2. `migrate` — one-shot migration runner using the app image
307	3. `api` — Gin backend with in-process scheduler jobs
308	
309	Do not split the backend into multiple microservices.
310	
311	## 6.1.2 Docker requirements
312	
313	Create:
314	
315	- `deploy/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build
316	- `deploy/docker-compose.yml`
317	- `.dockerignore`
318	- healthchecks
319	- pinned image versions
320	- non-root runtime user
321	
322	## 6.1.3 Persistent mounted volumes
323	
324	Mount local host directories for:
325	
326	- MySQL data
327	- app storage
328	- backups
329	- logs
330	- encryption keys
331	
332	Recommended host paths:
333	
334	- `./.data/mysql`
335	- `./.data/storage`
336	- `./.data/backups`
337	- `./.data/logs`
338	- `./.secrets`
339	
340	## 6.1.4 Runtime container paths
341	
342	Standardize and document paths such as:
343	
344	- `/var/lib/propertyops/storage`
345	- `/var/lib/propertyops/backups`
346	- `/var/log/propertyops`
347	- `/run/propertyops/keys`
348	
349	## 6.1.5 Docker image contents
350	
351	Compile and package real binaries:
352	
353	- `propertyops-api`
354	- `propertyops-migrate`
355	- `propertyops-healthcheck`
356	
357	## 6.1.6 Healthchecks
358	
359	Implement:
360	
361	- `GET /health/live`
362	- `GET /health/ready`
363	- a local container healthcheck binary under `cmd/healthcheck/main.go`
364	
365	Do not rely on `curl` being installed in the runtime image.
366	
367	---
368	
369	## Phase 2 — Entry points, config, and startup consistency
370	
371	Create explicit entry points:
372	
373	1. `cmd/api/main.go`
374	2. `cmd/migrate/main.go`
375	3. `cmd/healthcheck/main.go`
376	
377	## 6.2.1 Config requirements
378	
379	Centralize all environment/config loading in one package.
380	
381	Every env var must appear in all of these places:
382	
383	- config struct(s)
384	- `.env.example`
385	- `README.md`
386	- `deploy/docker-compose.yml` if relevant
387	
388	## 6.2.2 Minimum config areas
389	
390	- DB connection settings
391	- server port
392	- storage root
393	- backup root
394	- log root
395	- key directory
396	- bcrypt cost
397	- session timeouts
398	- encryption active key id
399	- scheduler intervals

1. `cmd/api/main.go`
2. `cmd/migrate/main.go`
3. `cmd/healthcheck/main.go`

## 6.2.1 Config requirements

Centralize all environment/config loading in one package.

Every env var must appear in all of these places:

- config struct(s)
- `.env.example`
- `README.md`
- `deploy/docker-compose.yml` if relevant

## 6.2.2 Minimum config areas

- DB connection settings
- server port
- storage root
- backup root
- log root
- key directory
- bcrypt cost
- session timeouts
- encryption active key id
- scheduler intervals

content.content:
400	- backup schedule settings
401	- retention settings
402	- notification retry settings
403	- anomaly ingestion allowlist
404	- default property timezone

407	
408	On startup, fail fast if:
409	
410	- config is invalid
411	- DB is unreachable
412	- key directory / active key is missing
413	- storage paths cannot be created or accessed
414	
415	Do not silently degrade security features.

641	## Phase 6 — Authorization and object-level access control
642	
643	This is one of the most important areas for the static audit.
644	
645	## 6.6.1 Implement and document an authorization matrix
646	
647	Create `docs/authorization-matrix.md` and encode the same rules in code.
648	
649	### Tenant
650	
651	- view own profile
652	- create own work orders
653	- view only their own unit/property work orders as allowed by business rules
654	- rate only their own completed work orders
655	- view only their own notifications
656	- create reports
657	- view only their own payments
658	
659	### Technician
660	
661	- view only assigned work orders within scoped properties
662	- accept/update assignments
663	- update progress and costs within allowed states
664	- view only necessary tenant info, masked by default
665	
666	### Property Manager
667	
668	- view/manage work orders for assigned properties
669	- reassign technician with required reason
670	- approve costs / completion
671	- oversee SLA
672	- mark payment intents paid
673	- initiate discrepancy workflows
674	- export PII only with explicit purpose
675	- view local reports and analytics for their properties
676	
677	### Compliance Reviewer
678	
679	- review report queue
680	- inspect evidence
681	- apply warning / rate limit / suspension
682	- resolve or dismiss reports
683	
684	### System Administrator
685	
686	- configuration
687	- audit log access
688	- backup and restore validation
689	- retention control
690	- key rotation
691	- health detail
692	- log query API
693	
694	## 6.6.2 Do not rely only on route-level authorization
695	
696	Add object-level checks inside services and repositories, for example:
697	
698	- tenant owns the target work order / unit / payment
699	- technician is assigned to the target work order
700	- manager belongs to the target property
701	- export endpoint requires explicit purpose
702	- admin/internal endpoints require privileged role
703	
704	Use consistent `403` or `404` behavior and document the rule.

1286	- Not Applicable
1287	
1288	Do not make runtime claims that have not been proven.

file.content:
- backup schedule settings
- retention settings
- notification retry settings
- anomaly ingestion allowlist
- default property timezone

## 6.2.3 Startup behavior

On startup, fail fast if:

- config is invalid
- DB is unreachable
- key directory / active key is missing
- storage paths cannot be created or accessed

Do not silently degrade security features.

---

## Phase 3 — Database schema and migrations

Use SQL migrations as the source of truth.

Do not rely only on GORM AutoMigrate.

## 6.3.1 Prompt-required tables

These must exist clearly in migrations:

- `users`
- `roles`
- `sessions`
- `tenant_profiles`
- `properties`
- `units`
- `work_orders`
- `work_order_events`
- `cost_items`
- `attachments`
- `notifications`
- `notification_receipts`
- `reports`
- `keywords_blacklist` or equivalent
- `risk_rules`
- `payments`
- `reconciliation_runs`

## 6.3.2 Additional support tables to add

Add these because they make the business rules real and auditable:

- `user_roles`
- `property_staff_assignments`
- `technician_skill_tags`
- `dispatch_cursors`
- `message_threads`
- `thread_messages`
- `notification_templates`
- `enforcement_actions`
- `saved_reports`
- `generated_reports`
- `audit_logs`
- `encryption_key_versions`
- `system_settings`
- `payment_approvals`

## 6.3.3 Explicit indexes and constraints

At minimum include:

- unique username
- work order index on `(property_id, status, created_at)`
- report index on `(target_type, target_id)`

Also include indexes and constraints for:

- unique role name
- unique session token hash
- unique notification receipt `(notification_id, user_id)`
- unique property staff assignment combinations
- unique dispatch cursor `(property_id, skill_tag)`
- work orders by assigned technician
- work orders by SLA due time
- payments by status/kind/date
- audit logs by resource type/resource id/date
- notifications by recipient/status/scheduled time

## 6.3.4 Data type rules

- money fields: `DECIMAL(12,2)`
- no float for money
- timestamps in UTC with explicit conversion rules where needed
- enums represented in a controlled way and validated in code

---

## Phase 4 — Shared infrastructure

Before domain features, build shared primitives.

## 6.4.1 Error model

Create a consistent application error shape with:

- machine-readable code
- human-readable message
- HTTP status
- optional field errors

## 6.4.2 Structured logging

Use structured logging with categories like:

- `auth`
- `work_order`
- `notification`
- `governance`
- `payment`
- `analytics`
- `backup`
- `security`
- `system`

Always include request ID, and actor ID when known.

Never log:

- passwords
- raw session tokens
- access codes
- decrypted PII
- encryption keys

## 6.4.3 Validation helpers

Implement reusable validators for:

- description length `20-2000`
- reassign reason `10-500`
- rating `1-5`
- feedback `<= 1000`
- decimals with two places
- date format `MM/DD/YYYY`
- 12-hour time format
- max attachments count
- allowed image MIME
- file signature
- export purpose requirement

## 6.4.4 Local storage service

Implement a storage service for:

- work order images
- report evidence
- generated CSV files
- backups

Requirements:

- deterministic directory layout
- SHA-256 hash calculation
- relative local path storage
- no remote URL abstractions

## 6.4.5 Crypto services

Implement:

1. password hashing using bcrypt cost >= 12 or Argon2id
2. AES-256-GCM field encryption with key versions
3. key rotation support and active key switching
4. job or service for re-encrypting older records as needed

Sensitive fields to encrypt at rest:

- access codes
- phone numbers if stored
- limited PII retained in profiles

---

## Phase 5 — Authentication, sessions, and role model

## 6.5.1 Roles must be explicit

Use roles as first-class data, not a generic admin boolean:

- Tenant
- Technician
- Property Manager
- Compliance Reviewer
- System Administrator

## 6.5.2 Auth endpoints

Under `/api/v1/auth` implement at least:

- `POST /login`
- `POST /logout`
- `GET /me`

Optional but useful:

- `GET /sessions/me`

## 6.5.3 Session model

Use DB-backed opaque sessions, not JWT-only authentication.

Recommended session attributes:

- session token hash
- created at
- last seen at
- idle expires at
- absolute expires at
- revoked at
- user agent / client metadata if useful

Rules:

- 30 minutes idle timeout
- 7 days max lifetime
- refresh idle timeout on activity
- never extend beyond absolute lifetime

## 6.5.4 Security middleware

Implement middleware for:

- authentication
- role checks
- suspension checks
- property scope checks
- request IDs
- structured request logging
- panic recovery

---

## Phase 6 — Authorization and object-level access control

This is one of the most important areas for the static audit.

## 6.6.1 Implement and document an authorization matrix

Create `docs/authorization-matrix.md` and encode the same rules in code.

### Tenant

- view own profile
- create own work orders
- view only their own unit/property work orders as allowed by business rules
- rate only their own completed work orders
- view only their own notifications
- create reports
- view only their own payments

### Technician

- view only assigned work orders within scoped properties
- accept/update assignments
- update progress and costs within allowed states
- view only necessary tenant info, masked by default

### Property Manager

- view/manage work orders for assigned properties
- reassign technician with required reason
- approve costs / completion
- oversee SLA
- mark payment intents paid
- initiate discrepancy workflows
- export PII only with explicit purpose
- view local reports and analytics for their properties

### Compliance Reviewer

- review report queue
- inspect evidence
- apply warning / rate limit / suspension
- resolve or dismiss reports

### System Administrator

- configuration
- audit log access
- backup and restore validation
- retention control
- key rotation
- health detail
- log query API

## 6.6.2 Do not rely only on route-level authorization

Add object-level checks inside services and repositories, for example:

- tenant owns the target work order / unit / payment
- technician is assigned to the target work order
- manager belongs to the target property
- export endpoint requires explicit purpose
- admin/internal endpoints require privileged role

Use consistent `403` or `404` behavior and document the rule.

---

## Phase 7 — Work Orders (core domain)

This is the central business module.

## 6.7.1 Required work order creation fields

Implement typed DTOs for creation with:

- description (required, `20-2000` chars)
- priority enum (`Low`, `Normal`, `High`, `Emergency`)
- up to 6 images
- preferred access window

Add structured support fields to make analytics and dispatch work cleanly:

- `issue_type`
- `tags`
- derived or explicit `skill_tag`

## 6.7.2 Image validation

For each attachment:

- max 5 MB
- JPEG or PNG only
- validate MIME type and file signature
- reject mismatched type/signature
- compute hash
- store locally

## 6.7.3 SLA assignment

On submission assign:

- Emergency: 4 hours
- High: 24 hours
- Normal: 72 hours
- Low: 5 business days

Persist:

- `sla_due_at`
- `sla_breached_at` (nullable)

## 6.7.4 Deterministic auto-dispatch

Implement deterministic round-robin dispatch by:

- `property_id`
- `skill_tag`

Use a `dispatch_cursors` table and a transaction/locking strategy so the logic is visibly deterministic.

Filter eligible technicians by:

- role = Technician
- active
- assigned to property
- skill tag match

If no matching technician exists:

- keep status `New`
- write event explaining why
- notify property manager

## 6.7.5 Reassignment

Allow managers to reassign only with:

- new technician id
- reason `10-500` chars

Write both:

- work order event
- global audit log entry

## 6.7.6 Status lifecycle

Use exactly this state model:

- `New`
- `Assigned`
- `In Progress`
- `Awaiting Approval`
- `Completed`
- `Archived`

Implement allowed transitions in one visible state machine.

## 6.7.7 Cost items

Support:

- labor/material type
- USD amount with two decimals
- responsibility attribution: `Tenant`, `Vendor`, `Property`
- notes
- created_by

## 6.7.8 Rating and feedback

After completion, allow tenant to submit:

- rating `1-5`
- optional feedback `<= 1000`

## 6.7.9 Append-only work order events

Write a work-order event for every meaningful change, including:

- created
- attachment stored
- SLA assigned
- auto-dispatch success/failure
- reassigned
- status changed
- cost added
- approval requested
- completed
- archived
- rated

---

## Phase 8 — Notifications and local message threads

Notifications are **in-app only**. No external delivery.

## 6.8.1 Required data model

Implement:

- `notification_templates`
- `notifications`
- `notification_receipts`
- `message_threads`
- `thread_messages`

## 6.8.2 Required capabilities

Support:

- templates
- scheduled sends
- event-triggered sends
- read receipts
- retry rules for internal delivery jobs

## 6.8.3 Suggested event-triggered notifications

At minimum:

- work order created
- work order assigned
- work order reassigned
- SLA breached
- approval requested
- work order completed
- report filed
- enforcement applied
- payment marked paid
- reconciliation generated

## 6.8.4 Message threads

Implement local threads and messages under Notifications so Governance can file reports against them.

## 6.8.5 Internal delivery job

Use in-process scheduler code that:

- picks pending notifications
- delivers internally
- retries per policy
- records job logs and audit entries

---

## Phase 9 — Governance, reports, moderation, and risk controls

This must be real functionality, not a thin report CRUD layer.

## 6.9.1 Reporting targets

Support reports against:

- tenant
- work order
- message thread

Each report needs:

- target type
- target id
- category enum
- description / rationale
- evidence attachments
- status

## 6.9.2 Manual review queue

Implement review statuses such as:

- `Open`
- `In Review`
- `Resolved`
- `Dismissed`

## 6.9.3 Enforcement actions

Implement actual enforcement records and logic for:

- warning
- rate limit
- suspension for 1 day
- suspension for 7 days
- suspension indefinite

## 6.9.4 Rate limiting

Implement DB-backed rate limiting for user-generated submission flows, for example:

- max 10 submissions per hour

Apply to at least:

- work order creation
- report creation
- thread message creation

## 6.9.5 Suspension enforcement

Suspended users must be blocked by the authorization / business layer from restricted actions.

## 6.9.6 Blacklist / risk rules

Do not leave these unused.

Use them to:

- flag risky or abusive content
- route content to elevated manual review
- support configurable moderation behavior

---

## Phase 10 — Offline payments and reconciliation

Payments must be fully local and must not depend on external processors.

## 6.10.1 Prohibited payment patterns

Do not implement:

- provider callbacks
- Stripe/PayPal/etc clients
- webhook processing
- remote payment confirmation logic

## 6.10.2 Payment model

Use `payments` as a local ledger with kinds such as:

- `intent`
- `settlement_posting`
- `makeup_posting`
- `reversal`

Important attributes include:

- tenant / unit / property linkage
- amount
- currency
- status
- created_by
- approved_by
- related payment references where needed
- expiration timestamp for intents

## 6.10.3 Intent flow

Implement:

- create payment intent
- expire intent after 30 minutes if not marked paid
- only authorized manager may mark it paid

## 6.10.4 Reconciliation runs

Implement daily reconciliation over local postings.

Persist in `reconciliation_runs`:

- run date
- status
- summary
- statement file path
- discrepancy count
- generated by

Generate local statement files, preferably CSV and/or JSON.

## 6.10.5 Discrepancy workflow

Support:

- make-up posting
- reversal

For discrepancy actions above `$500.00`:

- require dual approval
- require two distinct approvers
- prevent the same user from counting twice

This must be obvious in service code and tests.

---

## Phase 11 — Analytics and local reporting

Analytics must be computed from local event data only.

## 6.11.1 Required analytics endpoints

Under `/api/v1/analytics`, implement:

- popularity (most-requested issue types)
- conversion funnel (request -> assigned -> completed)
- retention (repeat requests per unit over 30/90 days)
- search-term analysis over work order tags
- quality metrics:
  - negative rating rate
  - report rate

## 6.11.2 Saved reports

Implement a saved-report model with:

- name
- owner
- filters JSON
- report type
- schedule
- output format

## 6.11.3 CSV export

Implement local CSV generation and file storage for generated reports.

Do not email or upload files externally.

## 6.11.4 Scheduled report generation

Use in-process scheduler code and also provide a manual trigger path or service entry for traceability.

---

## Phase 12 — Admin / operations features from the prompt

Do not skip these because they are easy to overlook.

## 6.12.1 Health checks

Implement:

1. minimal liveness endpoint
2. detailed readiness/health endpoint for privileged access

Detailed health should cover at least:

- DB connectivity
- storage directory availability
- backup freshness
- key rotation due status
- scheduler heartbeat

## 6.12.2 Log query API

Implement an admin-only log query capability over local logs, with filters such as:

- time range
- level
- category
- request ID
- actor ID

## 6.12.3 Daily encrypted backups

Implement backup generation that:

- packages DB dump and local artifacts as needed
- encrypts the backup locally
- writes checksums / manifest
- stores backup files under the local backup root

## 6.12.4 Restore validation

Implement restore validation logic that:

- decrypts backup
- validates manifest/checksums
- verifies dump/file presence and structure
- records result in audit / job logs

## 6.12.5 Retention controls

Support configurable defaults:

- financial records: 7 years
- message content: 2 years

## 6.12.6 PII export flow

Do not expose raw PII export casually.

Implement a controlled export path that requires:

- elevated role
- explicit purpose
- audit log entry
- local output file

Default API responses must remain masked.

## 6.12.7 Local anomaly monitoring / alert ingestion

Implement a reserved local integration endpoint protected by local-network allowlist logic.

No credentials, keys, or internet dependency should be required for this local interface.

---

## Phase 13 — Routes and DTOs must mirror the prompt

Route registration must clearly show these capability groups:

- Authentication
- Tenants
- Work Orders
- Notifications
- Payments
- Governance
- Analytics

Add related operational/admin routes in a clearly protected area.

All core request/response bodies must use typed DTOs with validation.

Do not use `map[string]any` for core business endpoints.

---

## Phase 14 — Testing plan

Tests are mandatory for audit credibility.

## 6.14.1 Unit tests

Write unit tests for at least:

- password hashing cost / verification
- session expiry logic
- encryption/decryption and key version usage
- PII masking
- preferred access window validation
- attachment count / size / MIME / signature validation
- SLA calculation
- 5 business day calculation
- deterministic round-robin dispatch
- work order state transitions
- reassign reason validation
- rating validation
- report creation validation
- rate-limit enforcement
- suspension enforcement
- payment intent expiration
- dual approval above $500
- reconciliation summary generation
- analytics aggregations
- backup manifest / checksum validation
- logging redaction

## 6.14.2 API / handler tests

Use Gin + `httptest` to verify at least:

- `401` unauthenticated
- `403` unauthorized
- `404` not found
- `409` conflict where relevant
- validation errors on bad input
- masked responses by default
- export endpoints require explicit purpose
- cross-tenant / cross-property access blocked
- admin-only routes protected
- anomaly ingestion restricted to local callers or allowlist

## 6.14.3 Integration-style flow tests

Add route + service tests for end-to-end business flows such as:

1. tenant creates work order
2. SLA assigned
3. auto-dispatch or no-tech event recorded
4. technician updates status
5. manager approves completion
6. tenant rates completed work order
7. report is filed and reviewed
8. payment intent is created and marked paid
9. discrepancy above $500 requires dual approval
10. analytics report generation writes local CSV metadata

## 6.14.4 Performance claims

Do not claim the p95 target is achieved unless actually measured.

If benchmarks or performance tests are added, mark them as:

- **Manual Verification Required**

---

## Phase 15 — Documentation package

## 6.15.1 Root README

The project `README.md` must include:

- project purpose
- offline-only runtime constraints
- architecture summary
- exact entry points
- migration command
- run command
- test command
- Docker build/up/down/log commands
- configuration explanation
- storage and volume layout
- route group summary
- manual verification notes

## 6.15.2 Required docs

Create:

- `docs/architecture.md`
- `docs/assumptions.md`
- `docs/requirement-matrix.md`
- `docs/authorization-matrix.md`
- `docs/test-matrix.md`
- `docs/security-model.md`
- `docs/api-overview.md` or `docs/openapi.yaml`
- `docs/backup-restore.md`
- `docs/manual-verification.md`
- `docs/verification-report.md`

## 6.15.3 Verification report structure

Structure `docs/verification-report.md` to mirror the audit categories:

1. documentation and static verifiability
2. delivery completeness
3. engineering and architecture quality
4. engineering details and professionalism
5. prompt understanding and requirement fit
6. frontend not applicable
7. security summary
8. tests and logging summary
9. manual verification required items

Use labels such as:

- Implemented
- Partially Implemented
- Manual Verification Required
- Not Applicable

Do not make runtime claims that have not been proven.

---

## Phase 16 — Docker documentation and consistency

Docker is part of the deliverable and must be documented clearly.

## 6.16.1 Required Docker documentation in README

Document real commands such as:

```bash
cp .env.example .env
mkdir -p ./.data/mysql ./.data/storage ./.data/backups ./.data/logs ./.secrets

docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml build
docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml up -d db
docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml run --rm migrate
docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml up -d api
```

Also document:

```bash
docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml logs -f api
docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml ps
docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml down
```

## 6.16.2 Compose consistency rules

Make sure the following align exactly:

- compose service names
- env variable names
- binary names
- mounted paths
- config defaults
- README commands

## 6.16.3 Docker static-review traps to avoid

Avoid these mistakes:

- compose references a missing binary
- README references a missing compose file
- app expects different paths from those mounted in compose
- secrets copied into image
- DB unnecessarily exposed publicly
- migration logic hidden only in shell scripts
- healthcheck references a missing command

---

## Phase 17 — Final static-audit cleanup pass

Before finishing, perform a repository-wide consistency check.

## 6.17.1 Check for common failure modes

1. README commands point to real files
2. every env var used in code is in `.env.example`
3. Docker files match the real repository
4. all prompt-required route groups exist
5. all prompt-required tables exist in migrations
6. handlers are registered and reachable from router setup
7. service logic exists behind the handlers
8. docs map cleanly to code and tests
9. object-level authorization exists, not only route-level guards
10. admin/internal routes are protected
11. PII is masked by default in normal responses
12. backups and restore validation are implemented, not only described
13. key rotation support exists in code
14. payments do not contain external provider logic
15. governance includes review queue + enforcement, not just report creation
16. analytics are not hardcoded or stubbed
17. core modules do not contain unresolved `TODO` / `FIXME`
18. tests cover core flows and security boundaries

---

# 7. Required data protection behavior

## 7.1 Mask PII by default

Default API responses must mask sensitive values such as phone numbers and access codes when applicable.

## 7.2 Explicit-purpose export

Any export of sensitive data must require:

- elevated role
- explicit purpose field
- audit log entry
- controlled export path / file generation

## 7.3 Sensitive logging rules

Never log:

- raw passwords
- raw session tokens
- access codes
- decrypted phone numbers
- encryption keys
- raw backup encryption secrets

---

# 8. Logging and audit expectations

## 8.1 Global audit log

Record important changes for:

- auth events
- session revocation
- work order creation and updates
- reassignment
- approvals
- enforcement actions
- payment state changes
- reconciliation runs
- backup generation and restore validation
- key rotation actions
- admin exports

## 8.2 Work-order-specific audit trail

Use append-only `work_order_events` for work-order actions.

## 8.3 Troubleshooting quality

Logs must support diagnosis through:

- request ID
- actor ID
- category
- event/action name
- result status
- resource identifiers where safe

---

# 9. Security review targets to pass

The reviewer will explicitly assess these areas. Make them easy to inspect.

## 9.1 Authentication entry points

Make login/logout/session handling clear and self-contained.

## 9.2 Route-level authorization

Use clearly named middleware and route group protection.

## 9.3 Object-level authorization

Enforce inside services for cross-tenant/cross-property access.

## 9.4 Function-level authorization

Sensitive actions like export, reconciliation approval, enforcement, backup validation, and key rotation must require privileged roles.

## 9.5 Tenant / user isolation

Tenants must not be able to access other tenants' work orders, notifications, or payments.

## 9.6 Admin / internal protection

Health detail, log query, backup management, restore validation, and system configuration endpoints must be protected.

---

# 10. Suggested route grouping

Organize routes approximately like this:

- `/api/v1/auth`
- `/api/v1/tenants`
- `/api/v1/work-orders`
- `/api/v1/notifications`
- `/api/v1/payments`
- `/api/v1/governance`
- `/api/v1/analytics`
- `/api/v1/admin`
- `/health/live`
- `/health/ready`

Keep route registration centralized in one obvious router file.

---

# 11. Recommended domain and service decomposition

This is a suggested module split. Equivalent clear structure is acceptable.

## 11.1 `auth`

- login/logout
- session issuance and validation
- auth middleware

## 11.2 `users` / `tenants` / `properties`

- user profiles
- tenant profiles
- property/unit relations
- staff assignment to properties

## 11.3 `workorders`

- create/update/query work orders
- state machine
- SLA assignment
- dispatch
- reassignment
- rating
- events
- cost items

## 11.4 `attachments`

- validation
- hashing
- storage
- metadata persistence

## 11.5 `notifications`

- templates
- message threads
- scheduled sends
- delivery jobs
- read receipts

## 11.6 `governance`

- reports
- review queue
- enforcement actions
- moderation helpers
- blacklist/risk rule evaluation

## 11.7 `payments`

- intents
- settlement postings
- discrepancy handling
- dual approvals
- reconciliation

## 11.8 `analytics`

- local aggregations
- saved reports
- scheduled report generation
- CSV export

## 11.9 `backups`, `health`, `logs`, `audit`, `security`

- operational/admin capabilities

---

# 12. Minimum API behavior expectations

## 12.1 Validation and error handling

Core endpoints must:

- validate inputs strictly
- return stable error shapes
- enforce enums and boundary conditions
- reject invalid file uploads cleanly

## 12.2 Authorization responses

Use consistent status codes and document the rule for `403` vs `404`.

## 12.3 Pagination/filtering

For list endpoints, include basic pagination/filtering where useful and test important cases.

## 12.4 Money handling

Represent money using fixed decimal strings / decimal types, never floats.

---

# 13. Testing matrix expectations

Create `docs/test-matrix.md` mapping requirements to tests.

For each important requirement or risk area, map:

- requirement / risk point
- test file(s)
- key assertions
- coverage status
- gap
- minimum future addition if not fully covered

This is important because the evaluator will perform a static test coverage audit.

---

# 14. What not to do

Do not:

- build a one-file monolith
- use demo-only in-memory persistence for core flows
- leave analytics hardcoded
- leave governance as report creation only
- model payments as generic CRUD without lifecycle rules
- expose debug/admin features without protection
- forget object-level authorization
- claim performance targets without measured evidence
- claim runtime success based on docs alone
- add third-party network dependencies
- include unrelated technology just to appear more advanced

---

# 15. Final handoff checklist

Before considering the project complete, verify all of the below are true.

## 15.1 Documentation and static verifiability

- [ ] root `README.md` exists and is coherent
- [ ] Docker startup/run/test/config instructions exist
- [ ] `.env.example` covers all used env vars
- [ ] route groups and entry points are statically obvious
- [ ] docs map prompt requirements to implementation

## 15.2 Delivery completeness

- [ ] all core functional areas from the business prompt are implemented
- [ ] project is a complete backend, not a fragment or example
- [ ] core flows are backed by real service logic

## 15.3 Engineering / architecture quality

- [ ] module boundaries are clear
- [ ] no major core logic pile-up in one file
- [ ] repository structure looks like a real application

## 15.4 Engineering details and professionalism

- [ ] validation exists for all important inputs
- [ ] logs are structured and useful
- [ ] errors are stable and readable
- [ ] sensitive logging is avoided

## 15.5 Requirement fit

- [ ] solution remains centered on offline-first property operations
- [ ] no major deviation from the business prompt
- [ ] no external-service substitution for required local features

## 15.6 Security

- [ ] authentication is implemented clearly
- [ ] route-level authorization exists
- [ ] object-level authorization exists
- [ ] tenant isolation is enforced
- [ ] admin/internal endpoints are protected
- [ ] PII is masked by default
- [ ] export requires purpose and audit

## 15.7 Tests and logging

- [ ] unit tests exist for core logic
- [ ] handler/API tests exist for auth and permissions
- [ ] high-risk flows are covered
- [ ] logs support troubleshooting

## 15.8 Docker

- [ ] `deploy/Dockerfile` exists and is multi-stage
- [ ] `deploy/docker-compose.yml` exists and is coherent
- [ ] image/binary/path names all match
- [ ] healthcheck is implemented
- [ ] migration container flow is documented
- [ ] local volume paths are documented and consistent

---

# 16. Final instruction block

Use this as your guiding principle throughout implementation:

> Build this as a production-shaped, Docker-first Go backend optimized for static review.
> The delivered repository must include a statically consistent multi-stage Dockerfile, Docker Compose deployment, migration container flow, persistent volume layout, healthchecks, environment examples, and exact README commands.
> Use a single-node Docker architecture with one backend container, one MySQL container, and one one-shot migration container.
> Keep all runtime behavior fully offline on a local network with no external third-party services or outbound dependencies.
> For every core requirement, provide schema/migrations, typed DTO validation, service logic, route registration, tests, and documentation.
> Use explicit role-based and object-level authorization, global audit logging, append-only work order events, local file storage, DB-backed sessions, AES-256 field encryption with key rotation support, and local-only payments, notifications, analytics, backups, and scheduled jobs.
> Structure the repository so a static reviewer can trace requirements from docs to Docker files to config to routes to services to schema to tests without guessing.

Let me start building. This is a massive project — I'll work through it systematically.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <version>3.2.5</version>
        <relativePath/>
    </parent>

    <groupId>com.campusfit</groupId>
    <artifactId>campusfit-api</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <name>CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations</name>
    <description>Offline-first CampusFit system with fitness goals, study planning, inbound receiving, and master data management</description>

    <properties>
        <java.version>17</java.version>
        <flyway.version>9.22.3</flyway.version>
        <jjwt.version>0.12.5</jjwt.version>
        <poi.version>5.2.5</poi.version>
        <opencsv.version>5.9</opencsv.version>
        <bouncycastle.version>1.77</bouncycastle.version>
    </properties>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- Spring Boot Starters -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-security</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Database -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.mysql</groupId>
            <artifactId>mysql-connector-j</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.flywaydb</groupId>
            <artifactId>flyway-core</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.flywaydb</groupId>
            <artifactId>flyway-mysql</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Excel/CSV Support -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.poi</groupId>
            <artifactId>poi-ooxml</artifactId>
            <version>${poi.version}</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.opencsv</groupId>
            <artifactId>opencsv</artifactId>
            <version>${opencsv.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Security / Crypto -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
            <artifactId>bcprov-jdk18on</artifactId>
            <version>${bouncycastle.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Jackson for JSON -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-datatype-jsr310</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Lombok -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Testing -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-security-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.h2database</groupId>
            <artifactId>h2</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <finalName>campusfit-api</finalName>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <excludes>
                        <exclude>
                            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                        </exclude>
                    </excludes>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <includes>
                        <include>**/*Test.java</include>
                        <include>**/*Tests.java</include>
                    </includes>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
</project>

# =============================================================================
# CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations — Environment Configuration
# =============================================================================
# Copy this file to .env and adjust values for your deployment.

# --- Database ---
MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=campusfit_root_secret
MYSQL_DATABASE=campusfit
MYSQL_USER=campusfit
MYSQL_PASSWORD=campusfit_secret
DB_HOST=db
DB_PORT=3306

# --- Server ---
SERVER_PORT=8080

# --- Storage Paths (container-internal) ---
STORAGE_ROOT=/var/lib/campusfit/storage
BACKUP_ROOT=/var/lib/campusfit/backups
LOG_ROOT=/var/log/campusfit
KEY_DIR=/run/campusfit/keys

# --- Security ---
BCRYPT_COST=12
SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=30
SESSION_MAX_LIFETIME_DAYS=7
LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD=5
LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES=15

# --- Encryption ---
ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE_KEY_ID=key-001
ENCRYPTION_ALGORITHM=AES-256-GCM

# --- Scheduler Intervals (seconds) ---
SCHEDULER_SESSION_CLEANUP_INTERVAL=300
SCHEDULER_NOTIFICATION_DELIVERY_INTERVAL=60
SCHEDULER_BACKUP_INTERVAL=86400
SCHEDULER_FITNESS_CHECK_INTERVAL=3600
SCHEDULER_PAYMENT_EXPIRY_INTERVAL=60
SCHEDULER_REPORT_GENERATION_INTERVAL=86400
SCHEDULER_SLOW_QUERY_LOG_INTERVAL=300

# --- Pagination ---
DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE=25
MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100

# --- Cache ---
CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=300

# --- Retention ---
EXPORT_RETENTION_DAYS=30
BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS=90

# --- Notification ---
NOTIFICATION_MAX_RETRIES=3
NOTIFICATION_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS=60

# --- Fitness ---
FITNESS_MISSED_CHECKIN_THRESHOLD=2
FITNESS_DEFAULT_ADJUSTMENT_PERCENT=10

# --- Inventory ---
QUANTITY_VARIANCE_PERCENT_THRESHOLD=2
QUANTITY_VARIANCE_ABSOLUTE_THRESHOLD=5

.git
.gitignore
.data/
.secrets/
*.md
!README.md
target/
*.log
*.iml
.idea/
.vscode/
node_modules/
frontend/node_modules/
frontend/dist/

# =============================================================================
# CampusFit API — Multi-stage Docker Build
# =============================================================================

# Stage 1: Build the Spring Boot application
FROM maven:3.9.6-eclipse-temurin-17 AS builder

WORKDIR /build
COPY pom.xml .
RUN mvn dependency:go-offline -B

COPY src/ src/
RUN mvn package -DskipTests -B

# Stage 2: Build the Vue.js frontend
FROM node:20-alpine AS frontend-builder

WORKDIR /frontend
COPY frontend/package.json frontend/package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm ci --production=false 2>/dev/null || npm install
COPY frontend/ .
RUN npm run build

# Stage 3: Runtime image
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine AS runtime

# Security: run as non-root user
RUN addgroup -S campusfit && adduser -S campusfit -G campusfit

# Create required directories
RUN mkdir -p /var/lib/campusfit/storage \
             /var/lib/campusfit/backups \
             /var/log/campusfit \
             /run/campusfit/keys \
             /app/static \
    && chown -R campusfit:campusfit /var/lib/campusfit /var/log/campusfit /run/campusfit /app

# Copy application JAR
COPY --from=builder /build/target/campusfit-api.jar /app/campusfit-api.jar

# Copy frontend build to static directory
COPY --from=frontend-builder /frontend/dist /app/static

# Switch to non-root user
USER campusfit
WORKDIR /app

# Expose port
EXPOSE 8080

# Health check using curl-free approach (Spring Boot Actuator)
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=60s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/health/live || exit 1

# Entry point
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "/app/campusfit-api.jar"]

# =============================================================================
# CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations — Docker Compose
# Single-node offline deployment: MySQL + Migrate + API
# =============================================================================
version: "3.9"

services:
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # MySQL Database
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  db:
    image: mysql:8.0.36
    container_name: campusfit-db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD}
      MYSQL_DATABASE: ${MYSQL_DATABASE}
      MYSQL_USER: ${MYSQL_USER}
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD}
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:3306:3306"   # bind to localhost only — not exposed publicly
    volumes:
      - ../.data/mysql:/var/lib/mysql
      - ./mysql/conf.d:/etc/mysql/conf.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mysqladmin", "ping", "-h", "localhost", "-u", "root", "-p${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 30s
    networks:
      - campusfit-net

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # One-shot migration runner
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  migrate:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: deploy/Dockerfile
    container_name: campusfit-migrate
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: migrate
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: 3306
      MYSQL_DATABASE: ${MYSQL_DATABASE}
      MYSQL_USER: ${MYSQL_USER}
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD}
    entrypoint: ["java", "-jar", "/app/campusfit-api.jar", "--spring.flyway.enabled=true", "--spring.main.web-application-type=none", "--spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=none"]
    restart: "no"
    networks:
      - campusfit-net

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # API Server (Spring Boot + Vue.js static files)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  api:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: deploy/Dockerfile
    container_name: campusfit-api
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: production
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: 3306
      MYSQL_DATABASE: ${MYSQL_DATABASE}
      MYSQL_USER: ${MYSQL_USER}
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD}
      SERVER_PORT: ${SERVER_PORT:-8080}
      STORAGE_ROOT: ${STORAGE_ROOT:-/var/lib/campusfit/storage}
      BACKUP_ROOT: ${BACKUP_ROOT:-/var/lib/campusfit/backups}
      LOG_ROOT: ${LOG_ROOT:-/var/log/campusfit}
      KEY_DIR: ${KEY_DIR:-/run/campusfit/keys}
      BCRYPT_COST: ${BCRYPT_COST:-12}
      SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES: ${SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES:-30}
      SESSION_MAX_LIFETIME_DAYS: ${SESSION_MAX_LIFETIME_DAYS:-7}
      LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD: ${LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD:-5}
      LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES: ${LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES:-15}
      ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE_KEY_ID: ${ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE_KEY_ID:-key-001}
      DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE: ${DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE:-25}
      MAX_PAGE_SIZE: ${MAX_PAGE_SIZE:-100}
      CACHE_TTL_SECONDS: ${CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:-300}
    volumes:
      - ../.data/storage:/var/lib/campusfit/storage
      - ../.data/backups:/var/lib/campusfit/backups
      - ../.data/logs:/var/log/campusfit
      - ../.secrets:/run/campusfit/keys:ro
    networks:
      - campusfit-net

networks:
  campusfit-net:
    driver: bridge

[mysqld]
# CampusFit MySQL Configuration — Offline Local Deployment

# Character set
character-set-server=utf8mb4
collation-server=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

# InnoDB settings
innodb_buffer_pool_size=256M
innodb_log_file_size=64M
innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit=1

# Slow query logging
slow_query_log=1
slow_query_log_file=/var/log/mysql/slow-query.log
long_query_time=2

# Connection limits
max_connections=100

# Binary logging for backup consistency
log_bin=mysql-bin
binlog_format=ROW
expire_logs_days=7

# Timezone
default-time-zone='+00:00'

[client]
default-character-set=utf8mb4

# =============================================================================
# CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations — Makefile
# =============================================================================

COMPOSE=docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml

.PHONY: help build up down logs migrate test clean dev frontend

help: ## Show available commands
	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*?## .*$$' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sort | awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*?## "}; {printf "\033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2}'

setup: ## Create required directories and copy env
	cp -n .env.example .env || true
	mkdir -p .data/mysql .data/storage .data/backups .data/logs .secrets

build: ## Build Docker images
	$(COMPOSE) build

up: ## Start all services
	$(COMPOSE) up -d

down: ## Stop all services
	$(COMPOSE) down

logs: ## Tail API logs
	$(COMPOSE) logs -f api

migrate: ## Run database migrations
	$(COMPOSE) run --rm migrate

db-up: ## Start only the database
	$(COMPOSE) up -d db

test: ## Run tests locally
	./mvnw test

clean: ## Remove data directories (DESTRUCTIVE)
	rm -rf .data/ .secrets/

ps: ## Show service status
	$(COMPOSE) ps

restart: ## Restart API service
	$(COMPOSE) restart api

dev: ## Run Spring Boot in dev mode locally
	./mvnw spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=dev

frontend-install: ## Install frontend dependencies
	cd frontend && npm install

frontend-dev: ## Run frontend dev server
	cd frontend && npm run dev

frontend-build: ## Build frontend for production
	cd frontend && npm run build

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"

         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <parent>

    <groupId>com.campusfit</groupId>
    <artifactId>campusfit-api</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <name>CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations</name>
    <description>Offline-first CampusFit system with fitness goals, study planning, inbound receiving, and master data management</description>

    <properties>
        <java.version>17</java.version>
        <flyway.version>9.22.3</flyway.version>
        <jjwt.version>0.12.5</jjwt.version>
        <poi.version>5.2.5</poi.version>
        <opencsv.version>5.9</opencsv.version>
        <bouncycastle.version>1.77</bouncycastle.version>
    </properties>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- Spring Boot Starters -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-security</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Database -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.mysql</groupId>
            <artifactId>mysql-connector-j</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.flywaydb</groupId>
            <artifactId>flyway-core</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.flywaydb</groupId>
            <artifactId>flyway-mysql</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Excel/CSV Support -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.poi</groupId>
            <artifactId>poi-ooxml</artifactId>
            <version>${poi.version}</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.opencsv</groupId>
            <artifactId>opencsv</artifactId>
            <version>${opencsv.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Security / Crypto -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
            <artifactId>bcprov-jdk18on</artifactId>
            <version>${bouncycastle.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Jackson for JSON -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-datatype-jsr310</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Lombok -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Testing -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-security-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.h2database</groupId>
            <artifactId>h2</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <finalName>campusfit-api</finalName>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <excludes>
                        <exclude>
                            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                        </exclude>
                    </excludes>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <includes>
                        <include>**/*Test.java</include>
                        <include>**/*Tests.java</include>
                    </includes>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
</project>

# =============================================================================
# CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations — Environment Configuration
# =============================================================================
# Copy this file to .env and adjust values for your deployment.

# --- Database ---
MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=campusfit_root_secret
MYSQL_DATABASE=campusfit
MYSQL_USER=campusfit
MYSQL_PASSWORD=campusfit_secret
DB_HOST=db
DB_PORT=3306

# --- Server ---
SERVER_PORT=8080

# --- Storage Paths (container-internal) ---
STORAGE_ROOT=/var/lib/campusfit/storage
BACKUP_ROOT=/var/lib/campusfit/backups
LOG_ROOT=/var/log/campusfit
KEY_DIR=/run/campusfit/keys

# --- Security ---
BCRYPT_COST=12
SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=30
SESSION_MAX_LIFETIME_DAYS=7
LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD=5
LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES=15

# --- Encryption ---
ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE_KEY_ID=key-001
ENCRYPTION_ALGORITHM=AES-256-GCM

# --- Scheduler Intervals (seconds) ---
SCHEDULER_SESSION_CLEANUP_INTERVAL=300
SCHEDULER_NOTIFICATION_DELIVERY_INTERVAL=60
SCHEDULER_BACKUP_INTERVAL=86400
SCHEDULER_FITNESS_CHECK_INTERVAL=3600
SCHEDULER_PAYMENT_EXPIRY_INTERVAL=60
SCHEDULER_REPORT_GENERATION_INTERVAL=86400
SCHEDULER_SLOW_QUERY_LOG_INTERVAL=300

# --- Pagination ---
DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE=25
MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100

# --- Cache ---
CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=300

# --- Retention ---
EXPORT_RETENTION_DAYS=30
BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS=90

# --- Notification ---
NOTIFICATION_MAX_RETRIES=3
NOTIFICATION_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS=60

# --- Fitness ---
FITNESS_MISSED_CHECKIN_THRESHOLD=2
FITNESS_DEFAULT_ADJUSTMENT_PERCENT=10

# --- Inventory ---
QUANTITY_VARIANCE_PERCENT_THRESHOLD=2
QUANTITY_VARIANCE_ABSOLUTE_THRESHOLD=5

# =============================================================================
# CampusFit API — Multi-stage Docker Build
# =============================================================================

# Stage 1: Build the Spring Boot application
FROM maven:3.9.6-eclipse-temurin-17 AS builder

WORKDIR /build
COPY pom.xml .
RUN mvn dependency:go-offline -B

COPY src/ src/
RUN mvn package -DskipTests -B

# Stage 2: Build the Vue.js frontend
FROM node:20-alpine AS frontend-builder

WORKDIR /frontend
COPY frontend/package.json frontend/package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm ci --production=false 2>/dev/null || npm install
COPY frontend/ .
RUN npm run build

# Stage 3: Runtime image
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine AS runtime

# Security: run as non-root user
RUN addgroup -S campusfit && adduser -S campusfit -G campusfit

# Create required directories
RUN mkdir -p /var/lib/campusfit/storage \
             /var/lib/campusfit/backups \
             /var/log/campusfit \
             /run/campusfit/keys \
             /app/static \
    && chown -R campusfit:campusfit /var/lib/campusfit /var/log/campusfit /run/campusfit /app

# Copy application JAR
COPY --from=builder /build/target/campusfit-api.jar /app/campusfit-api.jar

# Copy frontend build to static directory
COPY --from=frontend-builder /frontend/dist /app/static

# Switch to non-root user
USER campusfit
WORKDIR /app

# Expose port
EXPOSE 8080

# Health check using curl-free approach (Spring Boot Actuator)
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=60s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/health/live || exit 1

# Entry point
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "/app/campusfit-api.jar"]

server:
  port: ${SERVER_PORT:8080}

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mysql://${DB_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:3306}/${MYSQL_DATABASE:campusfit}?useSSL=false&allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true&serverTimezone=UTC&characterEncoding=utf8mb4
    username: ${MYSQL_USER:campusfit}
    password: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD:campusfit_secret}
    driver-class-name: com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver
    hikari:
      maximum-pool-size: 20
      minimum-idle: 5
      idle-timeout: 300000
      connection-timeout: 20000
      max-lifetime: 1200000

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: none
    show-sql: false
    properties:
      hibernate:
        dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.MySQLDialect
        format_sql: true
        jdbc:
          time_zone: UTC

  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration
    baseline-on-migrate: true
    validate-on-migrate: true

  jackson:
    serialization:
      write-dates-as-timestamps: false
    default-property-inclusion: non_null
    date-format: MM/dd/yyyy

  servlet:
    multipart:
      max-file-size: 5MB
      max-request-size: 30MB

# Application-specific configuration
app:
  security:
    bcrypt-cost: ${BCRYPT_COST:12}
    session-idle-timeout-minutes: ${SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES:30}
    session-max-lifetime-days: ${SESSION_MAX_LIFETIME_DAYS:7}
    lockout-threshold: ${LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD:5}
    lockout-duration-minutes: ${LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES:15}
  encryption:
    active-key-id: ${ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE_KEY_ID:key-001}
    algorithm: AES/GCM/NoPadding
    key-dir: ${KEY_DIR:.secrets}
  storage:
    root: ${STORAGE_ROOT:.data/storage}
    backup-root: ${BACKUP_ROOT:.data/backups}
    log-root: ${LOG_ROOT:.data/logs}
  pagination:
    default-page-size: ${DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE:25}
    max-page-size: ${MAX_PAGE_SIZE:100}
  cache:
    ttl-seconds: ${CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:300}
  retention:
    export-days: ${EXPORT_RETENTION_DAYS:30}
    backup-days: ${BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS:90}
  notification:
    max-retries: ${NOTIFICATION_MAX_RETRIES:3}

toolUseResult.content:
server:
  port: ${SERVER_PORT:8080}

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mysql://${DB_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:3306}/${MYSQL_DATABASE:campusfit}?useSSL=false&allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true&serverTimezone=UTC&characterEncoding=utf8mb4
    username: ${MYSQL_USER:campusfit}
    password: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD:campusfit_secret}
    driver-class-name: com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver
    hikari:
      maximum-pool-size: 20
      minimum-idle: 5
      idle-timeout: 300000
      connection-timeout: 20000
      max-lifetime: 1200000

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: none
    show-sql: false
    properties:
      hibernate:
        dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.MySQLDialect
        format_sql: true
        jdbc:
          time_zone: UTC

  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration
    baseline-on-migrate: true
    validate-on-migrate: true

  jackson:
    serialization:
      write-dates-as-timestamps: false
    default-property-inclusion: non_null
    date-format: MM/dd/yyyy

  servlet:
    multipart:
      max-file-size: 5MB
      max-request-size: 30MB

# Application-specific configuration
app:
  security:
    bcrypt-cost: ${BCRYPT_COST:12}
    session-idle-timeout-minutes: ${SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES:30}
    session-max-lifetime-days: ${SESSION_MAX_LIFETIME_DAYS:7}
    lockout-threshold: ${LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD:5}
    lockout-duration-minutes: ${LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES:15}
  encryption:
    active-key-id: ${ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE_KEY_ID:key-001}
    algorithm: AES/GCM/NoPadding
    key-dir: ${KEY_DIR:.secrets}
  storage:
    root: ${STORAGE_ROOT:.data/storage}
    backup-root: ${BACKUP_ROOT:.data/backups}
    log-root: ${LOG_ROOT:.data/logs}
  pagination:
    default-page-size: ${DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE:25}
    max-page-size: ${MAX_PAGE_SIZE:100}
  cache:
    ttl-seconds: ${CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:300}
  retention:
    export-days: ${EXPORT_RETENTION_DAYS:30}
    backup-days: ${BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS:90}
  notification:
    max-retries: ${NOTIFICATION_MAX_RETRIES:3}
    retry-delay-seconds: ${NOTIFICATION_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS:60}
  fitness:
    missed-checkin-threshold: ${FITNESS_MISSED_CHECKIN_THRESHOLD:2}
    default-adjustment-percent: ${FITNESS_DEFAULT_ADJUSTMENT_PERCENT:10}
  inventory:
    quantity-variance-percent-threshold: ${QUANTITY_VARIANCE_PERCENT_THRESHOLD:2}
    quantity-variance-absolute-threshold: ${QUANTITY_VARIANCE_ABSOLUTE_THRESHOLD:5}

# Logging configuration
logging:
  level:
    root: INFO
    com.campusfit: DEBUG
    org.hibernate.SQL: WARN
  pattern:
    console: "%d{ISO8601} [%thread] %-5level %logger{36} [%X{requestId}] [%X{actorId}] - %msg%n"
  file:
    name: ${LOG_ROOT:.data/logs}/campusfit.log

management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: when_authorized
