input.prompt:
You are building the database migration files for a CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations system using Spring Boot + Flyway + MySQL. The project root is at C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo and migrations go in src/main/resources/db/migration/.

Create the following Flyway migration files. Use proper SQL with explicit indexes, constraints, foreign keys, and NOT NULL where appropriate. Money fields use DECIMAL(12,2). Timestamps in UTC. Enums as VARCHAR with validation in code.

## V1__core_schema.sql
Tables for auth, users, roles:
- `roles` (id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL — values: REGULAR_USER, WAREHOUSE_STAFF, ADMINISTRATOR, created_at, updated_at)
- `users` (id, username VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL, password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, email VARCHAR(255), full_name VARCHAR(255), role_id FK to roles, status ENUM active/suspended/locked, failed_login_attempts INT DEFAULT 0, locked_until TIMESTAMP NULL, last_login_at TIMESTAMP NULL, created_at, updated_at)
- `sessions` (id, token_hash VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL, user_id FK to users, created_at, last_seen_at, idle_expires_at, absolute_expires_at, revoked_at TIMESTAMP NULL, user_agent VARCHAR(500), ip_address VARCHAR(45))
- `audit_logs` (id, actor_id BIGINT NULL, action VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, resource_type VARCHAR(100), resource_id BIGINT, detail JSON, ip_address VARCHAR(45), request_id VARCHAR(36), created_at) — INDEX on (resource_type, resource_id, created_at), INDEX on (actor_id, created_at)
- `encryption_key_versions` (id, key_id VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL, algorithm VARCHAR(50), created_at, rotated_at TIMESTAMP NULL, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE')

## V2__fitness_schema.sql
Tables for fitness goals module:
- `fitness_assessments` (id, user_id FK, height_ft INT, height_in INT, weight_lb DECIMAL(6,2), body_fat_percent DECIMAL(5,2) NULL, chest_in DECIMAL(5,2) NULL, waist_in DECIMAL(5,2) NULL, hips_in DECIMAL(5,2) NULL, bicep_in DECIMAL(5,2) NULL, assessment_date DATE NOT NULL, notes TEXT NULL, created_at, updated_at) — INDEX (user_id, assessment_date)
- `fitness_goals` (id, user_id FK, goal_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL — e.g. WEIGHT_LOSS, MUSCLE_GAIN, FLEXIBILITY, ENDURANCE, target_value DECIMAL(8,2), target_unit VARCHAR(20), duration_weeks INT NOT NULL, start_date DATE NOT NULL, end_date DATE NOT NULL, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE' — ACTIVE/PAUSED/COMPLETED/CANCELLED, current_value DECIMAL(8,2) NULL, created_at, updated_at) — INDEX (user_id, status)
- `goal_phases` (id, goal_id FK, phase_number INT NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(100), description TEXT NULL, start_week INT, end_week INT, target_value DECIMAL(8,2), status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING', created_at) — UNIQUE (goal_id, phase_number)
- `weekly_checkins` (id, goal_id FK, user_id FK, week_number INT NOT NULL, check_in_date DATE NOT NULL, current_value DECIMAL(8,2), notes TEXT NULL, adjustment_applied BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, adjustment_reason TEXT NULL, created_at) — UNIQUE (goal_id, week_number)

## V3__study_schema.sql
Tables for study/review planning:
- `academic_terms` (id, name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at)
- `schools` (id, name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL, code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at)
- `majors` (id, school_id FK, name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL, code VARCHAR(50), status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at) — UNIQUE (school_id, code)
- `classes` (id, major_id FK, term_id FK, name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL, code VARCHAR(50), instructor VARCHAR(200) NULL, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at)
- `courses` (id, class_id FK, name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL, code VARCHAR(50), description TEXT NULL, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at)
- `study_plans` (id, user_id FK, term_id FK, course_id FK, plan_name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL, plan_type VARCHAR(50) — STUDY/MEMORIZATION/REVIEW, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, daily_target_minutes INT, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', streak_count INT DEFAULT 0, longest_streak INT DEFAULT 0, created_at, updated_at) — INDEX (user_id, status)
- `study_completions` (id, plan_id FK, user_id FK, completion_date DATE NOT NULL, minutes_spent INT, topics_covered TEXT, notes TEXT NULL, completed BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE, created_at) — UNIQUE (plan_id, completion_date)
- `forgetting_points` (id, plan_id FK, user_id FK, topic VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL, description TEXT NULL, review_count INT DEFAULT 0, last_reviewed_at TIMESTAMP NULL, confidence_level INT DEFAULT 1 — 1-5, tagged_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, created_at, updated_at)
- `master_data_change_history` (id, entity_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, entity_id BIGINT NOT NULL, field_name VARCHAR(100), old_value TEXT, new_value TEXT, changed_by BIGINT FK users, effective_date DATE, change_type VARCHAR(20) — CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE, created_at) — INDEX (entity_type, entity_id, created_at)

## V4__inventory_schema.sql
Tables for inbound receiving workflow:
- `suppliers` (id, name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL, code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE, contact_info TEXT NULL, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at)
- `warehouse_locations` (id, code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(200), zone VARCHAR(50), aisle VARCHAR(20), rack VARCHAR(20), level_num VARCHAR(20), capacity INT, current_occupancy INT DEFAULT 0, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at)
- `products` (id, sku VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(300) NOT NULL, description TEXT NULL, category VARCHAR(100), unit_of_measure VARCHAR(20), weight_lb DECIMAL(8,2) NULL, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at)
- `inbound_receipts` (id, reference_number VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL, receipt_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL — PURCHASE/TRANSFER/RETURN, supplier_id FK NULL, status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT' — DRAFT/RECEIVING/INSPECTION/PUTAWAY/COMPLETED/REJECTED, received_by BIGINT FK users NULL, inspected_by BIGINT FK users NULL, completed_by BIGINT FK users NULL, expected_date DATE NULL, received_date DATE NULL, notes TEXT NULL, requires_supervisor_review BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, supervisor_reviewed_by BIGINT FK users NULL, supervisor_review_notes TEXT NULL, created_at, updated_at) — INDEX (status, created_at), INDEX (reference_number)
- `inbound_receipt_lines` (id, receipt_id FK, product_id FK, expected_quantity INT NOT NULL, received_quantity INT DEFAULT 0, accepted_quantity INT DEFAULT 0, rejected_quantity INT DEFAULT 0, variance_quantity INT DEFAULT 0, variance_percent DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 0, putaway_location_id FK warehouse_locations NULL, inspection_result VARCHAR(20) NULL — PASS/FAIL, inspection_notes TEXT NULL, reason_code VARCHAR(50) NULL, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING', created_at, updated_at) — INDEX (receipt_id)
- `inbound_receipt_events` (id, receipt_id FK, event_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, description TEXT, actor_id BIGINT FK users NULL, old_status VARCHAR(20), new_status VARCHAR(20), detail JSON NULL, created_at) — INDEX (receipt_id, created_at)
- `putaway_recommendations` (id, receipt_line_id FK, location_id FK warehouse_locations, priority INT DEFAULT 1, reason TEXT NULL, accepted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, created_at)
- `inventory_stock` (id, product_id FK, location_id FK warehouse_locations, quantity_on_hand INT DEFAULT 0, quantity_reserved INT DEFAULT 0, quantity_available INT DEFAULT 0, last_updated_at TIMESTAMP, created_at) — UNIQUE (product_id, location_id)

## V5__notifications_schema.sql
Tables for notifications:
- `notification_templates` (id, code VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL, subject VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL, body_template TEXT NOT NULL, category VARCHAR(50) — ANNOUNCEMENT/REMINDER/FOLLOW_UP, channel VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'IN_APP', status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE', created_at, updated_at)
- `notifications` (id, template_id FK NULL, recipient_id FK users, sender_id FK users NULL, subject VARCHAR(500), body TEXT NOT NULL, category VARCHAR(50), priority VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'NORMAL', status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING' — PENDING/SENT/DELIVERED/FAILED/CANCELLED, scheduled_at TIMESTAMP NULL, sent_at TIMESTAMP NULL, retry_count INT DEFAULT 0, next_retry_at TIMESTAMP NULL, reference_type VARCHAR(100) NULL, reference_id BIGINT NULL, created_at) — INDEX (recipient_id, status, scheduled_at), INDEX (status, next_retry_at)
- `notification_receipts` (id, notification_id FK, user_id FK, read_at TIMESTAMP NULL, delivered_at TIMESTAMP NULL, created_at) — UNIQUE (notification_id, user_id)
- `system_settings` (id, setting_key VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL, setting_value TEXT, description VARCHAR(500), updated_by BIGINT FK users NULL, updated_at)

## V6__partitioning_and_indexes.sql
Add MySQL partitioning for performance:
- Partition study_completions by year/month on completion_date (RANGE)
- Partition audit_logs by year/month on created_at (RANGE)
- Pre-aggregated table: `dashboard_fitness_summary` (id, user_id FK, goal_id FK, period VARCHAR(20), period_date DATE, total_checkins INT, avg_value DECIMAL(8,2), progress_percent DECIMAL(5,2), computed_at TIMESTAMP, created_at) — UNIQUE (user_id, goal_id, period, period_date)
- Pre-aggregated table: `dashboard_study_summary` (id, user_id FK, plan_id FK, period VARCHAR(20), period_date DATE, total_completions INT, total_minutes INT, avg_daily_minutes DECIMAL(8,2), current_streak INT, forgetting_points_count INT, computed_at TIMESTAMP, created_at) — UNIQUE (user_id, plan_id, period, period_date)
- Pre-aggregated table: `dashboard_inventory_summary` (id, period VARCHAR(20), period_date DATE, total_receipts INT, completed_receipts INT, rejected_receipts INT, avg_processing_hours DECIMAL(8,2), discrepancy_count INT, computed_at TIMESTAMP, created_at) — UNIQUE (period, period_date)

Create all 6 files with proper SQL DDL. Make sure every table has proper PRIMARY KEY, timestamps with DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, and appropriate indexes. Use InnoDB engine. Each file should be well-commented.

You are building shared infrastructure Java classes for a CampusFit Spring Boot application. The project root is C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo. Package base: com.campusfit.

Create the following files:

## 1. src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/exception/AppException.java
Application error with: code (String), message (String), httpStatus (int), fieldErrors (Map<String,String> nullable). Extend RuntimeException.

## 2. src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java
@RestControllerAdvice. Handle:
- AppException → return ErrorResponse with code, message, status, fieldErrors
- MethodArgumentNotValidException → 400 with field errors
- AccessDeniedException → 403
- Generic Exception → 500 with generic message (never expose internals)

## 3. src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/dto/ErrorResponse.java
Fields: timestamp (Instant), status (int), code (String), message (String), fieldErrors (Map<String,String>), requestId (String)

## 4. src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/dto/PageResponse.java
Generic wrapper: content (List<T>), page (int), size (int), totalElements (long), totalPages (int). Static factory from Spring Page.

## 5. src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/util/PiiMasker.java
Utility class with static methods:
- maskEmail(String) → "j***@example.com"
- maskPhone(String) → "***-***-1234" (last 4 visible)
- maskName(String) → "J*** D***"
- maskGeneric(String) → shows first and last char only

## 6. src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/validation/ValidationConstants.java
Constants: DESCRIPTION_MIN=20, DESCRIPTION_MAX=2000, REASON_MIN=10, REASON_MAX=500, RATING_MIN=1, RATING_MAX=5, FEEDBACK_MAX=1000, MAX_ATTACHMENTS=6, MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES=5*1024*1024, ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES={"image/jpeg","image/png"}, DATE_FORMAT="MM/dd/yyyy", DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE=25, MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100

## 7. src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/validation/FileValidator.java
@Component. Methods:
- validateImage(MultipartFile): validate MIME type, file signature (JPEG: FF D8 FF, PNG: 89 50 4E 47), size <= 5MB. Throws AppException on failure.
- computeSha256(InputStream): returns hex string

## 8. src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/util/DateUtils.java
- parseDate(String, "MM/dd/yyyy"): parse or throw
- isBusinessDay(LocalDate): Mon-Fri check
- addBusinessDays(LocalDate, int): skip weekends
- formatDate(LocalDate): to MM/dd/yyyy

## 9. src/main/java/com/campusfit/security/EncryptionService.java
@Service. AES-256-GCM field encryption:
- Properties: keyDir, activeKeyId from AppConfig
- @PostConstruct: load key from file (keyDir/activeKeyId.key — 32 bytes), fail fast if missing
- encrypt(String plaintext): returns "keyId:base64(iv+ciphertext)"
- decrypt(String ciphertext): parse keyId, load key, decrypt
- Fields: Map<String, SecretKey> keyCache
- Re-encrypt method: reEncrypt(String oldCiphertext) using current active key

## 10. src/main/java/com/campusfit/security/StorageService.java
@Service. Local file storage:
- store(String category, String filename, byte[] data): stores to storageRoot/category/yyyy/MM/filename, returns relative path
- load(String relativePath): returns byte[]
- computeHash(byte[]): SHA-256 hex
- delete(String relativePath): remove file
- Deterministic layout: storageRoot/category/yyyy/MM/dd/filename

## 11. src/main/java/com/campusfit/audit/entity/AuditLog.java
JPA @Entity, @Table(name="audit_logs"). Fields matching migration: id, actorId, action, resourceType, resourceId, detail (String, JSON column), ipAddress, requestId, createdAt.

## 12. src/main/java/com/campusfit/audit/repository/AuditLogRepository.java
JpaRepository<AuditLog, Long>. Query methods: findByResourceTypeAndResourceId, findByActorIdOrderByCreatedAtDesc, findByCreatedAtBetween with pagination.

## 13. src/main/java/com/campusfit/audit/service/AuditService.java
@Service. Methods:
- log(Long actorId, String action, String resourceType, Long resourceId, Object detail): creates AuditLog. Serializes detail to JSON. Gets requestId from MDC.
- query(String resourceType, Long resourceId, Pageable): returns Page<AuditLog>

## 14. src/main/java/com/campusfit/config/WebConfig.java
@Configuration implementing WebMvcConfigurer:
- Add RequestIdFilter as an interceptor or filter that sets MDC "requestId" (UUID) and "actorId" for every request
- Configure CORS for local development
- Serve static Vue.js files from /app/static

## 15. src/main/java/com/campusfit/config/RequestIdFilter.java
OncePerRequestFilter. Generates UUID requestId, puts in MDC, adds to response header X-Request-Id. Clears MDC on completion.

Make all classes properly annotated with Lombok (@Getter, @Setter, etc.) where appropriate. Use proper Spring stereotypes. All imports should be correct.

<summary>Agent "Create Flyway database migrations" completed</summary>
<result>All 33 tables are present across the 6 migration files. Here is a summary of what was created:

**6 Flyway migration files** in `C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo\src\main\resources\db\migration\`:

**V1__core_schema.sql** (5 tables) -- `roles`, `users`, `sessions`, `audit_logs`, `encryption_key_versions`. Includes seed data for the 3 roles (REGULAR_USER, WAREHOUSE_STAFF, ADMINISTRATOR). Foreign key from `users.role_id` to `roles`, and from `sessions.user_id` to `users`.

**V2__fitness_schema.sql** (4 tables) -- `fitness_assessments`, `fitness_goals`, `goal_phases`, `weekly_checkins`. All user-linked with FKs. Unique constraints on `(goal_id, phase_number)` and `(goal_id, week_number)`. Composite indexes on `(user_id, assessment_date)` and `(user_id, status)`.

**V3__study_schema.sql** (9 tables) -- `academic_terms`, `schools`, `majors`, `classes`, `courses`, `study_plans`, `study_completions`, `forgetting_points`, `master_data_change_history`. Full FK chain from schools through majors, classes, courses, to study plans. Unique constraint on `(plan_id, completion_date)` and `(school_id, code)`. Index on `(user_id, status)` for study plans and `(entity_type, entity_id, created_at)` for change history.

**V4__inventory_schema.sql** (8 tables) -- `suppliers`, `warehouse_locations`, `products`, `inbound_receipts`, `inbound_receipt_lines`, `inbound_receipt_events`, `putaway_recommendations`, `inventory_stock`. Inbound receipts have multiple FK references to `users` for received_by, inspected_by, completed_by, and supervisor_reviewed_by. Unique constraint on `(product_id, location_id)` for inventory stock. Composite indexes on `(status, created_at)` and `(receipt_id, created_at)`.

**V5__notifications_schema.sql** (4 tables) -- `notification_templates`, `notifications`, `notification_receipts`, `system_settings`. Composite index on `(recipient_id, status, scheduled_at)` for notification delivery queries and `(status, next_retry_at)` for retry processing. Unique constraint on `(notification_id, user_id)` for receipts.

**V6__partitioning_and_indexes.sql** (3 tables + 2 partition schemes) -- Applies RANGE partitioning on `study_completions` by `completion_date` and `audit_logs` by `created_at`, both with monthly partitions from Jan 2025 through Dec 2026 plus a catch-all future partition. Properly handles MySQL's requirement that partition columns be part of all unique keys by restructuring primary keys before partitioning. Creates 3 pre-aggregated dashboard tables: `dashboard_fitness_summary`, `dashboard_study_summary`, `dashboard_inventory_summary`, each with appropriate unique constraints on their composite period keys.

All tables use InnoDB engine, utf8mb4 charset with unicode_ci collation, `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` defaults, `ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` where appropriate, and properly named constraints (prefixed with `fk_`, `uq_`, `idx_`).</result>

input.prompt:
Build the complete Fitness Goals module for a CampusFit Spring Boot app at C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo. Package: com.campusfit.fitness. 

The module handles fitness assessments, goal management with phases, weekly check-ins, and dynamic adjustment rules. 

Create ALL of the following files:

## 1. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/FitnessAssessment.java
JPA entity for `fitness_assessments` table:
- id (Long, auto), userId (Long), heightFt (int), heightIn (int), weightLb (BigDecimal), bodyFatPercent (BigDecimal nullable), chestIn/waistIn/hipsIn/bicepIn (BigDecimal nullable), assessmentDate (LocalDate), notes (String), createdAt/updatedAt (Instant)

## 2. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/FitnessGoal.java
JPA entity for `fitness_goals` table:
- id, userId, goalType (enum: WEIGHT_LOSS, MUSCLE_GAIN, FLEXIBILITY, ENDURANCE, GENERAL), targetValue (BigDecimal), targetUnit (String), durationWeeks (int), startDate/endDate (LocalDate), status (enum: ACTIVE, PAUSED, COMPLETED, CANCELLED), currentValue (BigDecimal nullable), createdAt/updatedAt
- Add validation: durationWeeks > 0, startDate before endDate

## 3. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/GoalPhase.java
JPA entity for `goal_phases`:
- id, goalId (Long), phaseNumber (int), name (String), description, startWeek/endWeek (int), targetValue (BigDecimal), status (PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED), createdAt

## 4. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/WeeklyCheckin.java
JPA entity for `weekly_checkins`:
- id, goalId (Long), userId (Long), weekNumber (int), checkInDate (LocalDate), currentValue (BigDecimal), notes, adjustmentApplied (boolean), adjustmentReason, createdAt

## 5. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/repository/FitnessAssessmentRepository.java
JpaRepository with: findByUserIdOrderByAssessmentDateDesc, findLatestByUserId (native query)

## 6. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/repository/FitnessGoalRepository.java
JpaRepository with: findByUserIdAndStatus, findByUserId with Pageable, countByUserIdAndStatus

## 7. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/repository/GoalPhaseRepository.java
JpaRepository with: findByGoalIdOrderByPhaseNumberAsc

## 8. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/repository/WeeklyCheckinRepository.java
JpaRepository with: findByGoalIdOrderByWeekNumberAsc, findByGoalIdAndWeekNumber, countByGoalIdAndCreatedAtAfter

## 9. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/AssessmentRequest.java
Validated DTO: heightFt (@Min(1) @Max(8)), heightIn (@Min(0) @Max(11)), weightLb (@DecimalMin("50") @DecimalMax("1000")), optional body measurements, assessmentDate (String MM/dd/yyyy), notes

## 10. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/AssessmentResponse.java
Response DTO with all assessment fields + formatted dates

## 11. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/GoalRequest.java
Validated DTO: goalType (@NotBlank), targetValue (@NotNull @DecimalMin("0.01")), targetUnit, durationWeeks (@Min(1) @Max(104)), startDate (String MM/dd/yyyy)

## 12. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/GoalResponse.java
Response with goal fields, phases list, progress percentage, weeks remaining

## 13. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/CheckinRequest.java
Validated DTO: currentValue (@NotNull), notes (optional, max 1000)

## 14. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/CheckinResponse.java
Response with checkin data + whether adjustment was applied

## 15. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/DashboardResponse.java
Dashboard data: activeGoals count, currentAssessment summary, recentCheckins, overallProgress, streakInfo

## 16. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/FitnessService.java
@Service with full business logic:
- createAssessment(userId, AssessmentRequest): validate, save, audit log
- getAssessments(userId, Pageable): return paginated list, object-level auth (user can only see own)
- createGoal(userId, GoalRequest): validate dates, create goal with auto-generated phases (split duration into ~4 phases), compute end date, set initial target values per phase, audit log
- getGoals(userId, Pageable): paginated with object-level auth
- getGoalDetail(userId, goalId): with phases and recent checkins
- performCheckin(userId, goalId, CheckinRequest): validate week number, check consecutive misses, apply dynamic adjustment if 2+ consecutive checkins missed (recalculate weekly targets by appConfig.fitness.defaultAdjustmentPercent), record audit trail, update currentValue on goal
- getDashboard(userId): aggregate active goals, latest assessment, recent checkins, progress
- pauseGoal/resumeGoal/cancelGoal: state transitions with audit
- Dynamic adjustment rule: if 2 consecutive check-ins are missed, recalculate remaining weekly targets by reducing by adjustment percent, preserve audit trail of the adjustment

## 17. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/FitnessController.java
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/fitness"). Endpoints:
- POST /assessments — create assessment
- GET /assessments — list user's assessments (paginated)
- GET /assessments/latest — get latest assessment
- POST /goals — create goal
- GET /goals — list user's goals (paginated)
- GET /goals/{id} — goal detail with phases
- POST /goals/{id}/checkins — perform weekly checkin
- GET /goals/{id}/checkins — list checkins for goal
- PUT /goals/{id}/pause — pause goal
- PUT /goals/{id}/resume — resume goal
- PUT /goals/{id}/cancel — cancel goal
- GET /dashboard — fitness dashboard

All endpoints use AuthContext.getCurrentUserId() for object-level authorization. Use @Valid on request bodies. Return proper HTTP status codes (201 for create, 200 for get/update).

Make sure all imports are correct. Use Lombok annotations. Include proper @Transactional annotations on service methods.

- id, goalId (Long), phaseNumber (int), name (String), description, startWeek/endWeek (int), targetValue (BigDecimal), status (PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED), createdAt

## 4. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/WeeklyCheckin.java
JPA entity for `weekly_checkins`:
- id, goalId (Long), userId (Long), weekNumber (int), checkInDate (LocalDate), currentValue (BigDecimal), notes, adjustmentApplied (boolean), adjustmentReason, createdAt

## 5. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/repository/FitnessAssessmentRepository.java
JpaRepository with: findByUserIdOrderByAssessmentDateDesc, findLatestByUserId (native query)

## 6. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/repository/FitnessGoalRepository.java
JpaRepository with: findByUserIdAndStatus, findByUserId with Pageable, countByUserIdAndStatus

## 7. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/repository/GoalPhaseRepository.java
JpaRepository with: findByGoalIdOrderByPhaseNumberAsc

## 8. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/repository/WeeklyCheckinRepository.java
JpaRepository with: findByGoalIdOrderByWeekNumberAsc, findByGoalIdAndWeekNumber, countByGoalIdAndCreatedAtAfter

## 9. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/AssessmentRequest.java
Validated DTO: heightFt (@Min(1) @Max(8)), heightIn (@Min(0) @Max(11)), weightLb (@DecimalMin("50") @DecimalMax("1000")), optional body measurements, assessmentDate (String MM/dd/yyyy), notes

## 10. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/AssessmentResponse.java
Response DTO with all assessment fields + formatted dates

## 11. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/GoalRequest.java
Validated DTO: goalType (@NotBlank), targetValue (@NotNull @DecimalMin("0.01")), targetUnit, durationWeeks (@Min(1) @Max(104)), startDate (String MM/dd/yyyy)

## 12. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/GoalResponse.java
Response with goal fields, phases list, progress percentage, weeks remaining

## 13. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/CheckinRequest.java
Validated DTO: currentValue (@NotNull), notes (optional, max 1000)

## 14. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/CheckinResponse.java
Response with checkin data + whether adjustment was applied

## 15. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/DashboardResponse.java
Dashboard data: activeGoals count, currentAssessment summary, recentCheckins, overallProgress, streakInfo

## 16. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/FitnessService.java
@Service with full business logic:
- createAssessment(userId, AssessmentRequest): validate, save, audit log
- getAssessments(userId, Pageable): return paginated list, object-level auth (user can only see own)
- createGoal(userId, GoalRequest): validate dates, create goal with auto-generated phases (split duration into ~4 phases), compute end date, set initial target values per phase, audit log
- getGoals(userId, Pageable): paginated with object-level auth
- getGoalDetail(userId, goalId): with phases and recent checkins
- performCheckin(userId, goalId, CheckinRequest): validate week number, check consecutive misses, apply dynamic adjustment if 2+ consecutive checkins missed (recalculate weekly targets by appConfig.fitness.defaultAdjustmentPercent), record audit trail, update currentValue on goal
- getDashboard(userId): aggregate active goals, latest assessment, recent checkins, progress
- pauseGoal/resumeGoal/cancelGoal: state transitions with audit
- Dynamic adjustment rule: if 2 consecutive check-ins are missed, recalculate remaining weekly targets by reducing by adjustment percent, preserve audit trail of the adjustment

## 17. src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/FitnessController.java
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/fitness"). Endpoints:
- POST /assessments — create assessment
- GET /assessments — list user's assessments (paginated)
- GET /assessments/latest — get latest assessment
- POST /goals — create goal
- GET /goals — list user's goals (paginated)
- GET /goals/{id} — goal detail with phases
- POST /goals/{id}/checkins — perform weekly checkin
- GET /goals/{id}/checkins — list checkins for goal
- PUT /goals/{id}/pause — pause goal
- PUT /goals/{id}/resume — resume goal
- PUT /goals/{id}/cancel — cancel goal
- GET /dashboard — fitness dashboard

All endpoints use AuthContext.getCurrentUserId() for object-level authorization. Use @Valid on request bodies. Return proper HTTP status codes (201 for create, 200 for get/update).

Make sure all imports are correct. Use Lombok annotations. Include proper @Transactional annotations on service methods.

Build the complete Study/Review Planning module for a CampusFit Spring Boot app at C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo. Package: com.campusfit.study.

This module manages study plans tied to academic structures, daily completion tracking, forgetting points, streaks, reminders, and file export for cross-account migration.

Create ALL of the following files:

## 1. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java
JPA entity for `study_plans` table:
- id (Long auto), userId (Long), termId (Long), courseId (Long), planName (String), planType (enum: STUDY, MEMORIZATION, REVIEW), startDate/endDate (LocalDate), dailyTargetMinutes (int), status (enum: ACTIVE, PAUSED, COMPLETED, CANCELLED), streakCount (int default 0), longestStreak (int default 0), createdAt/updatedAt (Instant)

## 2. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyCompletion.java
JPA entity for `study_completions`:
- id, planId (Long), userId (Long), completionDate (LocalDate), minutesSpent (int), topicsCovered (String), notes, completed (boolean default true), createdAt

## 3. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/ForgettingPoint.java
JPA entity for `forgetting_points`:
- id, planId (Long), userId (Long), topic (String), description, reviewCount (int default 0), lastReviewedAt (Instant nullable), confidenceLevel (int 1-5), taggedAt (Instant), createdAt/updatedAt

## 4. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/repository/StudyPlanRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByUserIdAndStatus, findByUserId with Pageable, findByUserIdAndTermId

## 5. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/repository/StudyCompletionRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByPlanIdOrderByCompletionDateDesc, findByPlanIdAndCompletionDate, countByPlanIdAndCompletedTrue, findByUserIdAndCompletionDateBetween

## 6. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/repository/ForgettingPointRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByPlanIdOrderByConfidenceLevelAsc, findByUserIdAndConfidenceLevelLessThan, countByPlanId

## 7. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/StudyPlanRequest.java
Validated DTO: termId, courseId, planName (@NotBlank @Size(max=200)), planType (@NotBlank), startDate (String), endDate (String), dailyTargetMinutes (@Min(5) @Max(480))

## 8. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/StudyPlanResponse.java
Response with all plan fields + completionRate, currentStreak, totalMinutes, forgettingPointsCount

## 9. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/CompletionRequest.java
Validated: completionDate (String MM/dd/yyyy), minutesSpent (@Min(1)), topicsCovered, notes (optional max 1000)

## 10. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/CompletionResponse.java
Response with completion data + updated streak info

## 11. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/ForgettingPointRequest.java
Validated: topic (@NotBlank @Size(max=500)), description, confidenceLevel (@Min(1) @Max(5))

## 12. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/ForgettingPointResponse.java
Response with forgetting point data

## 13. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/StudyExportData.java
Export wrapper: plans list, completions list, forgettingPoints list, metadata (exportDate, userId, version)

## 14. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyService.java
@Service with business logic:
- createPlan(userId, StudyPlanRequest): validate dates, check term/course existence via masterdata, save, audit log
- getPlans(userId, Pageable): paginated, object-level auth
- getPlanDetail(userId, planId): with recent completions and forgetting points, object-level auth (user owns plan)
- recordCompletion(userId, planId, CompletionRequest): validate date not in future, prevent duplicate for same date, update streak (increment if consecutive day, reset if gap), update longestStreak, audit
- getCompletions(planId, Pageable): paginated completions
- addForgettingPoint(userId, planId, ForgettingPointRequest): save with taggedAt = now, audit
- reviewForgettingPoint(userId, pointId): increment reviewCount, update lastReviewedAt, optionally update confidenceLevel
- getForgettingPoints(planId, Pageable): sorted by confidence level ascending
- exportUserData(userId): gather all plans, completions, forgetting points into StudyExportData, write to JSON file in storage, require explicit purpose, audit log, return file path
- pausePlan/resumePlan/completePlan: state transitions with audit
- Streak calculation: check if previous day has completion. If yes, increment. If gap, reset to 1. Track longest streak.

## 15. src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyController.java
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/study"). Endpoints:
- POST /plans — create study plan
- GET /plans — list plans (paginated)
- GET /plans/{id} — plan detail
- POST /plans/{id}/completions — record daily completion
- GET /plans/{id}/completions — list completions
- POST /plans/{id}/forgetting-points — add forgetting point
- GET /plans/{id}/forgetting-points — list forgetting points
- PUT /plans/{id}/forgetting-points/{pointId}/review — mark reviewed
- PUT /plans/{id}/pause — pause
- PUT /plans/{id}/resume — resume
- PUT /plans/{id}/complete — mark complete
- POST /export — export all study data (requires purpose param)
- GET /dashboard — study dashboard (active plans, streaks, today's progress)

All endpoints use AuthContext for object-level auth. Use @Valid. Proper HTTP status codes.

Make sure all imports are correct. Use Lombok. Include @Transactional on service write methods.

<summary>Agent "Create shared infrastructure classes" completed</summary>
<result>All 15 files are present. Here is a summary of what was created:

**Created 15 shared infrastructure Java classes for the CampusFit Spring Boot application:**

1. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/exception/AppException.java`** -- RuntimeException subclass with code, message, httpStatus, and nullable fieldErrors map. Three constructors for different use cases.

2. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java`** -- @RestControllerAdvice handling AppException (dynamic status), MethodArgumentNotValidException (400 with field errors), AccessDeniedException (403), and generic Exception (500 with safe message). All responses include requestId from MDC.

3. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/dto/ErrorResponse.java`** -- Lombok @Builder DTO with timestamp (Instant), status, code, message, fieldErrors, requestId. Uses @JsonInclude(NON_NULL) to omit null fields.

4. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/dto/PageResponse.java`** -- Generic pagination wrapper with content, page, size, totalElements, totalPages. Static `from(Page<T>)` factory method.

5. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/util/PiiMasker.java`** -- Utility class with maskEmail ("j***@example.com"), maskPhone ("***-***-1234"), maskName ("J*** D***"), and maskGeneric (first+last char only).

6. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/validation/ValidationConstants.java`** -- All specified constants including DESCRIPTION_MIN/MAX, REASON_MIN/MAX, RATING_MIN/MAX, FEEDBACK_MAX, MAX_ATTACHMENTS, MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES (5MB), ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES (Set), DATE_FORMAT, DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE, MAX_PAGE_SIZE.

7. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/validation/FileValidator.java`** -- @Component validating MIME type, magic bytes (JPEG: FF D8 FF, PNG: 89 50 4E 47), and 5MB size limit. Includes computeSha256(InputStream) returning hex string.

8. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/common/util/DateUtils.java`** -- parseDate (MM/dd/yyyy with AppException on failure), isBusinessDay (Mon-Fri), addBusinessDays (skips weekends), formatDate.

9. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/security/EncryptionService.java`** -- @Service with AES-256-GCM. Loads 32-byte keys from AppConfig keyDir. encrypt returns "keyId:base64(iv+ciphertext)". decrypt parses keyId to load correct key. reEncrypt decrypts then re-encrypts with active key. ConcurrentHashMap keyCache. @PostConstruct fails fast if key missing.

10. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/security/StorageService.java`** -- @Service with deterministic layout storageRoot/category/yyyy/MM/dd/filename. Methods: store (creates dirs, writes file, returns relative path), load (reads bytes), computeHash (SHA-256 hex), delete. Uses forward slashes in returned paths.

11. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/audit/entity/AuditLog.java`** -- JPA @Entity matching the V1 migration audit_logs table: id, actorId, action, resourceType, resourceId, detail (TEXT), ipAddress, requestId, createdAt (with @PrePersist default).

12. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/audit/repository/AuditLogRepository.java`** -- JpaRepository with findByResourceTypeAndResourceId, findByActorIdOrderByCreatedAtDesc, findByCreatedAtBetween -- all with Pageable support.

13. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/audit/service/AuditService.java`** -- @Service with log() that serializes detail to JSON via ObjectMapper, reads requestId and ipAddress from MDC. query() delegates to repository.

14. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/config/WebConfig.java`** -- @Configuration implementing WebMvcConfigurer. Registers RequestIdFilter at highest precedence. CORS configured for localhost:3000 and localhost:5173. Static resources served from /app/static/.

15. **`src/main/java/com/campusfit/config/RequestIdFilter.java`** -- OncePerRequestFilter generating UUID requestId, storing it in MDC along with ipAddress and optional actorId. Sets X-Request-Id response header. Clears MDC in finally block.

All classes use proper Spring stereotypes, Lombok annotations where appropriate, and reference the existing `AppConfig` class for configuration values (encryption keyDir/activeKeyId, storage root).</result>

input.prompt:
Build the complete Inbound Receiving Workflow module for a CampusFit Spring Boot app at C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo. Package: com.campusfit.inventory.

This module implements a state-machine-driven receiving process: Draft → Receiving → Inspection → Putaway → Completed/Rejected. It includes quality inspection, putaway recommendations, and discrepancy handling with supervisor review.

Create ALL of the following files:

## 1. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/InboundReceipt.java
JPA entity for `inbound_receipts`:
- id (Long auto), referenceNumber (String unique), receiptType (enum: PURCHASE, TRANSFER, RETURN), supplierId (Long nullable), status (enum: DRAFT, RECEIVING, INSPECTION, PUTAWAY, COMPLETED, REJECTED), receivedBy/inspectedBy/completedBy (Long nullable), expectedDate/receivedDate (LocalDate nullable), notes (String), requiresSupervisorReview (boolean default false), supervisorReviewedBy (Long nullable), supervisorReviewNotes (String), createdAt/updatedAt (Instant)

## 2. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/InboundReceiptLine.java
JPA entity for `inbound_receipt_lines`:
- id, receiptId (Long), productId (Long), expectedQuantity (int), receivedQuantity (int), acceptedQuantity (int), rejectedQuantity (int), varianceQuantity (int), variancePercent (BigDecimal), putawayLocationId (Long nullable), inspectionResult (enum: PASS, FAIL nullable), inspectionNotes, reasonCode (String), status (enum: PENDING, RECEIVED, INSPECTED, PUTAWAY, COMPLETED), createdAt/updatedAt

## 3. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/InboundReceiptEvent.java
JPA entity for `inbound_receipt_events`:
- id, receiptId (Long), eventType (String), description (String), actorId (Long nullable), oldStatus/newStatus (String), detail (String JSON), createdAt

## 4. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/PutawayRecommendation.java
JPA entity for `putaway_recommendations`:
- id, receiptLineId (Long), locationId (Long), priority (int), reason (String), accepted (boolean default false), createdAt

## 5. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/InventoryStock.java
JPA entity for `inventory_stock`:
- id, productId (Long), locationId (Long), quantityOnHand (int), quantityReserved (int), quantityAvailable (int), lastUpdatedAt (Instant), createdAt

## 6. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/Product.java
JPA entity for `products`:
- id, sku (String unique), name, description, category, unitOfMeasure, weightLb (BigDecimal nullable), status, createdAt/updatedAt

## 7. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/Supplier.java
JPA entity for `suppliers`:
- id, name, code (String unique), contactInfo, status, createdAt/updatedAt

## 8. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/WarehouseLocation.java
JPA entity for `warehouse_locations`:
- id, code (String unique), name, zone, aisle, rack, levelNum, capacity (int), currentOccupancy (int default 0), status, createdAt/updatedAt

## 9. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/repository/InboundReceiptRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByStatus, findByReferenceNumber, findByStatusIn with Pageable, countByStatus

## 10. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/repository/InboundReceiptLineRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByReceiptId, findByReceiptIdAndStatus

## 11. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/repository/InboundReceiptEventRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByReceiptIdOrderByCreatedAtAsc

## 12. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/repository/ProductRepository.java
JpaRepository: findBySku, findByCategory with Pageable, findByStatusAndNameContaining

## 13. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/repository/WarehouseLocationRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByCode, findByZone, findByStatusAndCurrentOccupancyLessThan

## 14. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/repository/InventoryStockRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByProductId, findByLocationId, findByProductIdAndLocationId

## 15. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/repository/PutawayRecommendationRepository.java
JpaRepository: findByReceiptLineIdOrderByPriorityAsc

## 16. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/ReceiptCreateRequest.java
Validated: referenceNumber (@NotBlank), receiptType (@NotBlank), supplierId (nullable), expectedDate (String), notes, lines (List of LineRequest)

## 17. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/LineRequest.java
Validated: productId (@NotNull), expectedQuantity (@Min(1))

## 18. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/ReceiveRequest.java
For recording received quantities: lines (List) each with lineId, receivedQuantity

## 19. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/InspectionRequest.java
For recording inspection: lineId, inspectionResult (PASS/FAIL), inspectionNotes, reasonCode (required if FAIL)

## 20. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/PutawayRequest.java
For accepting putaway: lineId, locationId (or accept recommendation)

## 21. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/ReceiptResponse.java
Full receipt response with lines, events, status history

## 22. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/DiscrepancyResponse.java
Discrepancy detail: lineId, expected, received, variance, variancePercent, requiresSupervisorReview, reasonCode

## 23. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/service/ReceivingService.java
@Service implementing the state machine:
- createReceipt(ReceiptCreateRequest): create in DRAFT status, create lines, record event, audit log
- startReceiving(receiptId): DRAFT→RECEIVING transition, validate, record event
- recordQuantities(receiptId, ReceiveRequest): update line quantities, calculate variances, check thresholds (>2% or >5 units → requiresSupervisorReview=true), record events for each line
- performInspection(receiptId, List<InspectionRequest>): RECEIVING→INSPECTION, record pass/fail per line, if any fail → can move to REJECTED
- generatePutawayRecommendations(receiptId): INSPECTION→PUTAWAY, for each passed line find best location (by zone, available capacity), create PutawayRecommendation records
- acceptPutaway(receiptId, List<PutawayRequest>): accept recommendations or override, update inventory_stock, record events
- completeReceipt(receiptId): PUTAWAY→COMPLETED, validate all lines resolved, update inventory, record event
- rejectReceipt(receiptId, reason): any state → REJECTED, record reason
- supervisorReview(receiptId, approved, notes): handle supervisor review for discrepancies
- getReceipt(receiptId): full detail with lines and events
- listReceipts(status, Pageable): paginated list
- getDiscrepancies(receiptId): list lines with variances

State machine rules:
- DRAFT → RECEIVING (only)
- RECEIVING → INSPECTION
- INSPECTION → PUTAWAY (if all inspected) or REJECTED (if critical failures)
- PUTAWAY → COMPLETED (if all putaway accepted)
- Any → REJECTED
- Completed/Rejected are terminal

Variance thresholds from AppConfig: quantityVariancePercentThreshold (default 2%), quantityVarianceAbsoluteThreshold (default 5 units). If exceeded, requiresSupervisorReview = true.

All state transitions MUST:
1. Validate current status allows transition
2. Record InboundReceiptEvent with old/new status
3. Write audit log entry
4. Throw AppException if invalid transition

## 24. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/service/InventoryService.java
@Service for stock management:
- getStockByProduct(productId): return stock across all locations
- getStockByLocation(locationId): return all stock at location
- adjustStock(productId, locationId, quantityDelta): atomic update
- getStockSummary(): aggregate stock counts

## 25. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/controller/InventoryController.java
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/inventory"). Endpoints:
- POST /receipts — create receipt (WAREHOUSE_STAFF, ADMINISTRATOR)
- GET /receipts — list receipts with optional status filter
- GET /receipts/{id} — receipt detail
- PUT /receipts/{id}/start-receiving — transition to RECEIVING
- POST /receipts/{id}/receive — record quantities
- POST /receipts/{id}/inspect — perform inspection
- POST /receipts/{id}/putaway — generate recommendations then accept
- POST /receipts/{id}/accept-putaway — accept putaway locations
- PUT /receipts/{id}/complete — complete receipt
- PUT /receipts/{id}/reject — reject receipt
- POST /receipts/{id}/supervisor-review — supervisor review
- GET /receipts/{id}/discrepancies — view discrepancies
- GET /receipts/{id}/events — event history
- GET /stock — stock summary
- GET /stock/product/{productId} — stock by product
- GET /stock/location/{locationId} — stock by location

Role checks: require WAREHOUSE_STAFF or ADMINISTRATOR for all inventory endpoints. Use AuthContext for authorization.

All imports must be correct. Use Lombok. Include @Transactional on write methods. Use com.campusfit.common.exception.AppException for errors. Use com.campusfit.audit.service.AuditService for audit logging. Use com.campusfit.config.AppConfig for configuration values. Use com.campusfit.security.AuthContext for auth context.

This module implements a state-machine-driven receiving process: Draft → Receiving → Inspection → Putaway → Completed/Rejected. It includes quality inspection, putaway recommendations, and discrepancy handling with supervisor review.

## 1. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/InboundReceipt.java
JPA entity for `inbound_receipts`:
- id (Long auto), referenceNumber (String unique), receiptType (enum: PURCHASE, TRANSFER, RETURN), supplierId (Long nullable), status (enum: DRAFT, RECEIVING, INSPECTION, PUTAWAY, COMPLETED, REJECTED), receivedBy/inspectedBy/completedBy (Long nullable), expectedDate/receivedDate (LocalDate nullable), notes (String), requiresSupervisorReview (boolean default false), supervisorReviewedBy (Long nullable), supervisorReviewNotes (String), createdAt/updatedAt (Instant)

## 2. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/InboundReceiptLine.java
JPA entity for `inbound_receipt_lines`:
- id, receiptId (Long), productId (Long), expectedQuantity (int), receivedQuantity (int), acceptedQuantity (int), rejectedQuantity (int), varianceQuantity (int), variancePercent (BigDecimal), putawayLocationId (Long nullable), inspectionResult (enum: PASS, FAIL nullable), inspectionNotes, reasonCode (String), status (enum: PENDING, RECEIVED, INSPECTED, PUTAWAY, COMPLETED), createdAt/updatedAt

## 3. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/InboundReceiptEvent.java
JPA entity for `inbound_receipt_events`:

## 4. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/PutawayRecommendation.java
JPA entity for `putaway_recommendations`:
- id, receiptLineId (Long), locationId (Long), priority (int), reason (String), accepted (boolean default false), createdAt

## 5. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/InventoryStock.java
JPA entity for `inventory_stock`:
- id, productId (Long), locationId (Long), quantityOnHand (int), quantityReserved (int), quantityAvailable (int), lastUpdatedAt (Instant), createdAt

## 6. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/Product.java

- id, sku (String unique), name, description, category, unitOfMeasure, weightLb (BigDecimal nullable), status, createdAt/updatedAt

## 7. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/entity/Supplier.java

- id, name, code (String unique), contactInfo, status, createdAt/updatedAt

## 14. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/repository/InventoryStockRepository.java

## 16. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/ReceiptCreateRequest.java

Validated: productId (@NotNull), expectedQuantity (@Min(1))

## 18. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/ReceiveRequest.java
For recording received quantities: lines (List) each with lineId, receivedQuantity

## 19. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/InspectionRequest.java
For recording inspection: lineId, inspectionResult (PASS/FAIL), inspectionNotes, reasonCode (required if FAIL)

## 20. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/PutawayRequest.java
For accepting putaway: lineId, locationId (or accept recommendation)

Full receipt response with lines, events, status history

## 22. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/dto/DiscrepancyResponse.java
Discrepancy detail: lineId, expected, received, variance, variancePercent, requiresSupervisorReview, reasonCode

## 23. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/service/ReceivingService.java
@Service implementing the state machine:
- createReceipt(ReceiptCreateRequest): create in DRAFT status, create lines, record event, audit log
- startReceiving(receiptId): DRAFT→RECEIVING transition, validate, record event
- recordQuantities(receiptId, ReceiveRequest): update line quantities, calculate variances, check thresholds (>2% or >5 units → requiresSupervisorReview=true), record events for each line
- performInspection(receiptId, List<InspectionRequest>): RECEIVING→INSPECTION, record pass/fail per line, if any fail → can move to REJECTED
- generatePutawayRecommendations(receiptId): INSPECTION→PUTAWAY, for each passed line find best location (by zone, available capacity), create PutawayRecommendation records
- acceptPutaway(receiptId, List<PutawayRequest>): accept recommendations or override, update inventory_stock, record events
- completeReceipt(receiptId): PUTAWAY→COMPLETED, validate all lines resolved, update inventory, record event
- rejectReceipt(receiptId, reason): any state → REJECTED, record reason
- supervisorReview(receiptId, approved, notes): handle supervisor review for discrepancies
- getReceipt(receiptId): full detail with lines and events
- listReceipts(status, Pageable): paginated list
- getDiscrepancies(receiptId): list lines with variances

State machine rules:
- DRAFT → RECEIVING (only)
- RECEIVING → INSPECTION
- INSPECTION → PUTAWAY (if all inspected) or REJECTED (if critical failures)
- PUTAWAY → COMPLETED (if all putaway accepted)
- Any → REJECTED
- Completed/Rejected are terminal

Variance thresholds from AppConfig: quantityVariancePercentThreshold (default 2%), quantityVarianceAbsoluteThreshold (default 5 units). If exceeded, requiresSupervisorReview = true.

All state transitions MUST:
1. Validate current status allows transition
2. Record InboundReceiptEvent with old/new status
3. Write audit log entry
4. Throw AppException if invalid transition

## 24. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/service/InventoryService.java
@Service for stock management:
- getStockByProduct(productId): return stock across all locations
- getStockByLocation(locationId): return all stock at location
- adjustStock(productId, locationId, quantityDelta): atomic update
- getStockSummary(): aggregate stock counts

## 25. src/main/java/com/campusfit/inventory/controller/InventoryController.java
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/inventory"). Endpoints:
- POST /receipts — create receipt (WAREHOUSE_STAFF, ADMINISTRATOR)
- GET /receipts — list receipts with optional status filter
- GET /receipts/{id} — receipt detail
- PUT /receipts/{id}/start-receiving — transition to RECEIVING
- POST /receipts/{id}/receive — record quantities
- POST /receipts/{id}/inspect — perform inspection
- POST /receipts/{id}/putaway — generate recommendations then accept
- POST /receipts/{id}/accept-putaway — accept putaway locations
- PUT /receipts/{id}/complete — complete receipt
- PUT /receipts/{id}/reject — reject receipt
- POST /receipts/{id}/supervisor-review — supervisor review
- GET /receipts/{id}/discrepancies — view discrepancies
- GET /receipts/{id}/events — event history
- GET /stock — stock summary
- GET /stock/product/{productId} — stock by product
- GET /stock/location/{locationId} — stock by location

Role checks: require WAREHOUSE_STAFF or ADMINISTRATOR for all inventory endpoints. Use AuthContext for authorization.

input.prompt:
Build the complete Master Data Management module for a CampusFit Spring Boot app at C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo. Package: com.campusfit.masterdata.

This module manages academic master data (terms, schools, majors, classes, courses) with bulk Excel/CSV import/export, inline validation errors, duplicate merging, and change history with effective dates.

Create ALL of the following files:

## 1. Entities (src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/entity/)

### AcademicTerm.java
JPA entity for `academic_terms`: id, name (String), startDate/endDate (LocalDate), status (ACTIVE/INACTIVE), createdAt/updatedAt

### School.java
JPA entity for `schools`: id, name, code (unique), status, createdAt/updatedAt

### Major.java
JPA entity for `majors`: id, schoolId (Long), name, code, status, createdAt/updatedAt

### ClassEntity.java
JPA entity for `classes` (table name "classes"): id, majorId (Long), termId (Long), name, code, instructor, status, createdAt/updatedAt

### Course.java
JPA entity for `courses`: id, classId (Long), name, code, description, status, createdAt/updatedAt

### MasterDataChangeHistory.java
JPA entity for `master_data_change_history`: id, entityType, entityId (Long), fieldName, oldValue, newValue, changedBy (Long), effectiveDate (LocalDate), changeType (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE), createdAt

## 2. Repositories (src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/repository/)

### AcademicTermRepository.java - findByStatus, findByName
### SchoolRepository.java - findByCode, findByStatus, existsByCode
### MajorRepository.java - findBySchoolId, findBySchoolIdAndCode, existsBySchoolIdAndCode
### ClassRepository.java - findByMajorId, findByTermId, findByMajorIdAndTermId
### CourseRepository.java - findByClassId, findByCode
### MasterDataChangeHistoryRepository.java - findByEntityTypeAndEntityIdOrderByCreatedAtDesc, findByChangedByOrderByCreatedAtDesc

## 3. DTOs (src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/dto/)

### TermRequest.java - Validated: name (@NotBlank @Size(max=100)), startDate/endDate (String MM/dd/yyyy)
### SchoolRequest.java - Validated: name (@NotBlank @Size(max=200)), code (@NotBlank @Size(max=50))
### MajorRequest.java - Validated: schoolId (@NotNull), name, code
### ClassRequest.java - Validated: majorId, termId, name, code, instructor
### CourseRequest.java - Validated: classId, name, code, description
### MergeRequest.java - sourceId (@NotNull), targetId (@NotNull) — merge source into target
### ImportResult.java - totalRows, successCount, errorCount, errors (List of ImportError with row, field, message)
### MasterDataResponse.java - Generic wrapper: id, name, code, status, createdAt, updatedAt, entityType
### ChangeHistoryResponse.java - entityType, entityId, fieldName, oldValue, newValue, changedBy, effectiveDate, changeType, createdAt

## 4. Service (src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataService.java)
@Service with:
- CRUD for each entity type (terms, schools, majors, classes, courses)
- Each create/update/delete records a MasterDataChangeHistory entry
- Effective dates in MM/DD/YYYY format stored as LocalDate
- Delete validation: block deletion when entity is referenced by study plans (check via repository count queries)
- Merge duplicates: mergeEntity(entityType, sourceId, targetId) — update all references from source to target, mark source as INACTIVE, record MERGE change history preserving both IDs
- getChangeHistory(entityType, entityId, Pageable): paginated change history
- Versioning: each update stores the effective date and old/new values

## 5. Service (src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/ImportExportService.java)
@Service with:
- importFromCsv(entityType, InputStream): parse CSV rows, validate each row, create/update entities, return ImportResult with per-row errors
- importFromExcel(entityType, InputStream): parse XLSX using Apache POI, validate, create/update, return ImportResult
- exportToCsv(entityType): query all entities, write CSV to storage, return file path
- exportToExcel(entityType): query all entities, write XLSX using POI, return file path
- Validation rules per entity type (non-null name, valid codes, dates, references exist)
- Inline error reporting: each row that fails validation is reported with row number, field name, error message

## 6. Controller (src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/MasterDataController.java)
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/masterdata"). Endpoints:

Terms:
- POST /terms, GET /terms, GET /terms/{id}, PUT /terms/{id}, DELETE /terms/{id}

Schools:
- POST /schools, GET /schools, GET /schools/{id}, PUT /schools/{id}, DELETE /schools/{id}

Majors:
- POST /majors, GET /majors, GET /majors/{id}, PUT /majors/{id}, DELETE /majors/{id}

Classes:
- POST /classes, GET /classes, GET /classes/{id}, PUT /classes/{id}, DELETE /classes/{id}

Courses:
- POST /courses, GET /courses, GET /courses/{id}, PUT /courses/{id}, DELETE /courses/{id}

Import/Export (ADMINISTRATOR only):
- POST /import/{entityType}?format=csv|excel — upload file, return ImportResult
- GET /export/{entityType}?format=csv|excel — download exported file

Merge (ADMINISTRATOR only):
- POST /merge/{entityType} — merge duplicates

Change History:
- GET /history/{entityType}/{entityId} — paginated change history

Authorization: Read operations allow REGULAR_USER and ADMINISTRATOR. Write/import/export/merge require ADMINISTRATOR. Use AuthContext for role checks. Use com.campusfit.audit.service.AuditService for audit logging. Use com.campusfit.common.exception.AppException for errors.

Use proper Lombok annotations, @Transactional on write methods, correct imports.

input.prompt:
Build the complete Notifications module for a CampusFit Spring Boot app at C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo. Package: com.campusfit.notifications.

This module implements a unified notification center with announcements, reminders, follow-ups, read receipts, and delivery status. In-app only in offline mode.

Create ALL of the following files:

## 1. Entities (src/main/java/com/campusfit/notifications/entity/)

### NotificationTemplate.java
JPA entity for `notification_templates`: id, code (String unique), subject, bodyTemplate (String TEXT), category (enum: ANNOUNCEMENT, REMINDER, FOLLOW_UP), channel (String default "IN_APP"), status (ACTIVE/INACTIVE), createdAt/updatedAt

### Notification.java
JPA entity for `notifications`: id, templateId (Long nullable), recipientId (Long), senderId (Long nullable), subject, body (String TEXT), category (String), priority (enum: LOW, NORMAL, HIGH, URGENT), status (enum: PENDING, SENT, DELIVERED, FAILED, CANCELLED), scheduledAt (Instant nullable), sentAt (Instant nullable), retryCount (int default 0), nextRetryAt (Instant nullable), referenceType (String nullable), referenceId (Long nullable), createdAt

### NotificationReceipt.java
JPA entity for `notification_receipts`: id, notificationId (Long), userId (Long), readAt (Instant nullable), deliveredAt (Instant nullable), createdAt. Unique constraint on (notificationId, userId)

## 2. Repositories (src/main/java/com/campusfit/notifications/repository/)

### NotificationTemplateRepository.java - findByCode, findByCategory, findByStatus
### NotificationRepository.java - findByRecipientIdAndStatusOrderByCreatedAtDesc (Pageable), findByStatusAndScheduledAtBefore, findByStatusAndNextRetryAtBefore, countByRecipientIdAndStatus
### NotificationReceiptRepository.java - findByNotificationIdAndUserId, findByUserIdAndReadAtIsNull, countByUserIdAndReadAtIsNull

## 3. DTOs (src/main/java/com/campusfit/notifications/dto/)

### SendNotificationRequest.java
Validated: recipientId (@NotNull), subject (@NotBlank @Size(max=500)), body (@NotBlank), category, priority, scheduledAt (String, optional — if set, schedule for later), referenceType/referenceId (optional)

### NotificationResponse.java
id, subject, body, category, priority, status, sentAt, readAt (from receipt), createdAt, senderName

### NotificationListResponse.java (extends PageResponse concept)
notifications (List<NotificationResponse>), unreadCount (long), totalCount (long)

### TemplateRequest.java
Validated: code (@NotBlank), subject (@NotBlank), bodyTemplate (@NotBlank), category

## 4. Service (src/main/java/com/campusfit/notifications/service/NotificationService.java)
@Service with:
- sendNotification(SendNotificationRequest): create notification, create receipt for recipient, if no scheduledAt then immediately mark as SENT, audit log
- sendFromTemplate(String templateCode, Long recipientId, Map<String,String> variables): load template, replace {{variable}} placeholders in body, send
- scheduleNotification(SendNotificationRequest): create with status PENDING and scheduledAt
- markAsRead(Long notificationId, Long userId): set readAt on receipt, audit
- markAllAsRead(Long userId): mark all unread notifications as read
- getNotifications(Long userId, Pageable): paginated list for user, include read status from receipts, object-level auth (users see only their own)
- getUnreadCount(Long userId): count unread
- cancelNotification(Long notificationId): mark CANCELLED if still PENDING
- sendBulkAnnouncement(String subject, String body, List<Long> recipientIds): create notification for each recipient

### Event-triggered notifications (called from other services):
- notifyFitnessCheckinReminder(Long userId, Long goalId)
- notifyStudyReminder(Long userId, Long planId)
- notifyReceiptStatusChange(Long receiptId, String newStatus, Long actorId)
- notifyDiscrepancyFound(Long receiptId, Long supervisorId)
- notifyMasterDataChange(String entityType, Long entityId, String changeType)

## 5. Service (src/main/java/com/campusfit/notifications/service/NotificationDeliveryJob.java)
@Component with @Scheduled methods:
- processScheduledNotifications(): every 60 seconds, find PENDING notifications with scheduledAt <= now, mark as SENT, update sentAt
- retryFailedNotifications(): every 60 seconds, find FAILED notifications with nextRetryAt <= now and retryCount < maxRetries, attempt re-delivery, increment retryCount, set nextRetryAt if still failing
- Both methods use @Transactional and log structured entries

## 6. Controller (src/main/java/com/campusfit/notifications/controller/NotificationController.java)
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/notifications"). Endpoints:
- GET / — list notifications for current user (paginated, includes unread count)
- GET /{id} — notification detail (object-level auth: only recipient can view)
- POST /send — send notification (ADMINISTRATOR only for direct send)
- POST /announce — bulk announcement (ADMINISTRATOR only)
- PUT /{id}/read — mark as read
- PUT /read-all — mark all as read
- PUT /{id}/cancel — cancel scheduled notification
- GET /unread-count — get unread count
- GET /templates — list templates (ADMINISTRATOR only)
- POST /templates — create template (ADMINISTRATOR only)

Use AuthContext for object-level auth. Use AuditService for audit. Use AppConfig for retry settings. Proper @Valid, HTTP status codes, Lombok, @Transactional.

- GET /study?userId={id} — study analytics (admin or self)

id, settingKey, settingValue, description, updatedBy, updatedAt

id, username, email (masked), fullName (masked), role, status, lastLoginAt, createdAt

Validated: startTime, endTime (String), level (optional), category (optional), requestId (optional), actorId (optional Long)

### src/main/java/com/campusfit/admin/dto/LogEntry.java
timestamp, level, category, message, requestId, actorId

slowQueryCount, avgResponseTime, activeUsers, activeSessions, dbConnectionPoolStatus

### src/main/java/com/campusfit/admin/service/AdminService.java
@Service:
- getSystemSettings(): list all system_settings
- updateSystemSetting(key, value): update setting, audit log
- getUsers(Pageable): paginated user list with masked PII by default
- suspendUser(userId, reason): set status to SUSPENDED, revoke all sessions, audit
- unsuspendUser(userId): set status to ACTIVE, audit
- deleteAccount(userId): hard-delete PII (username→anonymized, email→null, fullName→null), retain de-identified audit events, audit log
- queryLogs(LogQueryRequest): read from log files, filter by criteria, return paginated entries
- getPerformanceMetrics(): query for slow query count, active sessions count, DB pool status
- exportPii(userId, purpose): require ADMINISTRATOR role + explicit purpose, export user PII to file, audit log with purpose

### src/main/java/com/campusfit/admin/controller/AdminController.java
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/admin"):
- GET /settings — list settings
- PUT /settings/{key} — update setting
- GET /users — list users (masked PII)
- PUT /users/{id}/suspend — suspend user
- PUT /users/{id}/unsuspend — unsuspend user
- DELETE /users/{id} — delete account (hard-delete PII, keep audit)
- POST /users/{id}/export — export PII (requires purpose param)
- POST /logs/query — query logs
- GET /performance — performance metrics screen
- GET /audit-logs — paginated audit logs
- POST /backup — trigger backup
- POST /backup/validate — validate backup

All ADMINISTRATOR only. Use AuthContext.requireRole(ADMINISTRATOR).

## Module 3: Backup (com.campusfit.backup)

### src/main/java/com/campusfit/backup/service/BackupService.java
@Service:
- createBackup(): generate MySQL dump command string (for documentation — actual dump runs via shell), package metadata as JSON manifest (tables, row counts, timestamp, checksum), encrypt manifest with EncryptionService, save to backup root, audit log
- validateBackup(String backupPath): read manifest, verify checksums, verify file presence, record validation result, audit log
- listBackups(): list backup files from backup root directory
- cleanupOldBackups(): delete backups older than retention period
- getBackupStatus(): latest backup info, freshness check

### src/main/java/com/campusfit/backup/service/BackupScheduler.java
@Component with @Scheduled:
- @Scheduled(cron = "0 0 3 * * *") — daily at 3 AM
- Run createBackup(), then cleanupOldBackups()
- Structured logging

Use proper Lombok, @Transactional, correct imports. Use com.campusfit.security.AuthContext, com.campusfit.audit.service.AuditService, com.campusfit.config.AppConfig, com.campusfit.common.exception.AppException, com.campusfit.security.EncryptionService, com.campusfit.security.StorageService, com.campusfit.common.util.PiiMasker.

- `dto/CheckinResponse.java` -- Includes `adjustmentApplied` flag and reason

  - Goal creation with auto-generated phases (~4 phases: Foundation, Building, Intensify, Peak), evenly distributed weeks, incremental target values
  - Check-in with automatic week number calculation, duplicate detection, consecutive miss detection, and **dynamic adjustment rule**: when 2+ consecutive check-ins are missed, remaining phase targets are reduced by `appConfig.fitness.defaultAdjustmentPercent` (default 10%)
  - Dashboard aggregation: active goals count, latest assessment, recent checkins per goal, average progress, streak calculation

Create all Vue.js view components for the CampusFit application at C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t100\repo\frontend\src\views\. The app uses Vue 3 Composition API with `<script setup>`, Pinia stores, and the API modules already created in ../api/. No UI framework — use plain CSS classes already defined in App.vue (card, btn, btn-primary, btn-success, btn-danger, form-group, grid, grid-2, grid-3, stat-value, stat-label, status-badge, page-title, table, pagination, error-msg).

Create ALL of the following view files:

## 1. views/auth/LoginView.vue
Login form with username/password. On submit, call auth store login(), redirect to /. Show error on failure. Link to register.

## 2. views/auth/RegisterView.vue
Registration form: username, password, email, fullName. On submit, call auth store register(), redirect to /. Show errors. Link to login.

## 3. views/dashboard/DashboardView.vue
Role-based dashboard:
- REGULAR_USER: show fitness goals summary (active goals, latest checkin), study plans summary (active plans, current streak, today's progress), recent notifications
- WAREHOUSE_STAFF: show inventory summary (pending receipts, today's completed, discrepancies needing review)
- ADMINISTRATOR: show all above + system stats (total users, active sessions), quick links to admin pages
Use cards with stat-value/stat-label. Fetch data from respective APIs on mount.

## 4. views/fitness/FitnessView.vue
Fitness overview: latest assessment card, goals list (cards showing goal type, target, progress %, status badge, duration). Button to create assessment, create goal. Each goal card links to /fitness/goals/:id.

## 5. views/fitness/AssessmentView.vue
Form to create fitness assessment: height (ft + in dropdowns), weight (lb input), optional body measurements (body fat %, chest, waist, hips, bicep), assessment date, notes. Submit calls fitnessApi.createAssessment(). Show success/error.

## 6. views/fitness/NewGoalView.vue
Form: goal type (select: Weight Loss, Muscle Gain, Flexibility, Endurance, General), target value + unit, duration in weeks, start date. Example descriptions like "Lose 12 lb in 12 weeks". Submit creates goal, redirects to goal detail.

## 7. views/fitness/GoalDetailView.vue
Goal detail page: goal info card (type, target, duration, status, progress bar), phase milestones (list of phases with status indicators), weekly checkins list (table with week, date, value, notes, adjustment badge). Checkin form at top if goal is ACTIVE (current value input + notes). Action buttons: Pause/Resume/Cancel based on status.

## 8. views/study/StudyView.vue
Study overview: active plans list (cards with plan name, type badge, course, streak indicator as fire emoji + count, completion rate bar, forgetting points count). Button to create new plan, button to export data.

## 9. views/study/NewPlanView.vue
Form: select term, select course (cascading: term→school→major→class→course using masterdata API), plan name, plan type (Study/Memorization/Review), start date, end date, daily target minutes. Submit creates plan.

## 10. views/study/PlanDetailView.vue
Plan detail: info card, daily completion form (date, minutes spent, topics, notes), completions calendar/list, forgetting points section (list with topic, confidence level as stars, review count, button to mark reviewed, form to add new). Streak display. Action buttons.

## 11. views/inventory/InventoryView.vue
Inventory overview: receipt status summary (counts by status as stat cards), receipts table (reference, type, status badge, date, actions). Status filter tabs (All/Draft/Receiving/Inspection/Putaway/Completed/Rejected). Button to create new receipt.

## 12. views/inventory/NewReceiptView.vue
Form: reference number, receipt type (Purchase/Transfer/Return), supplier (select), expected date, notes. Line items section: add rows with product (select/search by SKU), expected quantity. Submit creates receipt.

## 13. views/inventory/ReceiptDetailView.vue
Full receipt workflow view — adapts based on status:
- DRAFT: show info + lines, button to start receiving
- RECEIVING: show lines with quantity input fields, button to submit quantities
- INSPECTION: show lines with pass/fail toggle, notes, reason code if fail, submit inspection
- PUTAWAY: show recommendations (location suggestions), accept/override for each line
- COMPLETED/REJECTED: read-only summary
Show discrepancies section (highlighted lines with variance > threshold). Event history timeline. Supervisor review section if needed.

## 14. views/masterdata/MasterDataView.vue
Tabbed view for managing: Terms, Schools, Majors, Classes, Courses. Each tab has:
- Table listing entities with name, code, status, actions (edit, delete)
- Create form (modal or inline)
- Edit form
- Import button (upload CSV/Excel file)
- Export button (download CSV/Excel)
- Merge duplicates button (select source + target)
- View change history (expandable per entity)
Use masterdataApi for all operations. Show ImportResult errors inline.

## 15. views/notifications/NotificationsView.vue
Notification center: unread count badge at top, "Mark all read" button. List of notifications (card per notification: subject bold, body preview, time ago, read/unread indicator, category badge). Click to expand full body and mark as read. Pagination.

## 16. views/admin/AdminView.vue
Admin dashboard: system stats cards (total users, active sessions, pending receipts, backup status), quick action cards (User Management, Audit Logs, Performance, Backup, System Settings). Each links to sub-page.

## 17. views/admin/UserManagementView.vue
Users table: username, email (masked), role, status badge, last login, actions (suspend/unsuspend, delete with confirmation, export PII with purpose prompt). Pagination.

## 18. views/admin/AuditLogsView.vue
Audit log viewer: filter form (date range, actor, action, resource type), table (timestamp, actor, action, resource, detail preview). Pagination. Each row expandable for full detail JSON.

## 19. views/admin/PerformanceView.vue
Performance dashboard: slow query count card, active sessions card, DB connection pool status. Auto-refresh toggle. Placeholder for charts.

Each view should:
- Use `<script setup>` with `import { ref, onMounted } from 'vue'`
- Import relevant API modules
- Handle loading states (show "Loading..." text)
- Handle errors (show error-msg div)
- Be fully functional with proper data binding
- Use the CSS classes from App.vue (no additional style blocks needed unless minor tweaks)

<output-file>C:\Users\fraol\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\C--Users-fraol-Documents-A-w2t100-repo\09a3683a-1fdb-4b2a-a004-d985b52c6a5a\tasks\a3c9b855995f809a5.output</output-file>

<summary>Agent "Build notifications module" completed</summary>
<result>All 12 files have been created and verified. Here is the summary of what was built:

---

**Notifications module created** -- 12 files across 5 packages under `com.campusfit.notifications`.

### Entities (3 files)
- **`entity/NotificationTemplate.java`** -- JPA entity for `notification_templates` table with enums `TemplateCategory` (ANNOUNCEMENT, REMINDER, FOLLOW_UP) and `TemplateStatus` (ACTIVE, INACTIVE). Fields: id, code (unique), subject, bodyTemplate (TEXT), category, channel (default "IN_APP"), status, createdAt/updatedAt with `@PrePersist`/`@PreUpdate` lifecycle hooks.
- **`entity/Notification.java`** -- JPA entity for `notifications` table with enums `Priority` (LOW, NORMAL, HIGH, URGENT) and `Status` (PENDING, SENT, DELIVERED, FAILED, CANCELLED). Fields: id, templateId (nullable), recipientId, senderId (nullable), subject, body (TEXT), category, priority, status, scheduledAt, sentAt, retryCount (default 0), nextRetryAt, referenceType, referenceId, createdAt.
- **`entity/NotificationReceipt.java`** -- JPA entity for `notification_receipts` table with `@UniqueConstraint` on (notification_id, user_id). Fields: id, notificationId, userId, readAt, deliveredAt, createdAt.

### Repositories (3 files)
- **`repository/NotificationTemplateRepository.java`** -- `findByCode`, `findByCategory`, `findByStatus`
- **`repository/NotificationRepository.java`** -- `findByRecipientIdAndStatusOrderByCreatedAtDesc` (Pageable), `findByRecipientIdOrderByCreatedAtDesc` (Pageable), `findByStatusAndScheduledAtBefore`, `findByStatusAndNextRetryAtBefore`, `countByRecipientIdAndStatus`
- **`repository/NotificationReceiptRepository.java`** -- `findByNotificationIdAndUserId`, `findByUserIdAndReadAtIsNull`, `countByUserIdAndReadAtIsNull`

### DTOs (4 files)
- **`dto/SendNotificationRequest.java`** -- Validated with `@NotNull` recipientId, `@NotBlank @Size(max=500)` subject, `@NotBlank` body, optional category/priority/scheduledAt/referenceType/referenceId
- **`dto/NotificationResponse.java`** -- Builder with id, subject, body, category, priority, status, sentAt, readAt, createdAt, senderName
- **`dto/NotificationListResponse.java`** -- Builder wrapping List of responses plus unreadCount, totalCount, page, size, totalPages
- **`dto/TemplateRequest.java`** -- Validated with `@NotBlank` code/subject/bodyTemplate, optional category

### Services (2 files)
- **`service/NotificationService.java`** -- Full service with:
  - `sendNotification` -- creates notification + receipt, immediate SENT or scheduled PENDING
  - `sendFromTemplate` -- loads template by code, replaces `{{variable}}` placeholders, sends
  - `scheduleNotification` -- creates with PENDING status and scheduledAt
  - `markAsRead` / `markAllAsRead` -- sets readAt on receipts
  - `getNotifications` -- paginated list with read status from receipts, object-level auth via `AuthContext.requireOwner`
  - `getNotificationDetail` -- single notification with object-level auth
  - `getUnreadCount` -- count of unread receipts
  - `cancelNotification` -- marks PENDING notification as CANCELLED
  - `sendBulkAnnouncement` -- creates notification per recipient
  - 5 event-triggered methods: `notifyFitnessCheckinReminder`, `notifyStudyReminder`, `notifyReceiptStatusChange`, `notifyDiscrepancyFound`, `notifyMasterDataChange`
  - Template CRUD: `createTemplate`, `listTemplates`
  - All mutations use `AuditService.log()` for audit trails
  - Sender name resolution from `UserRepository`

- **`service/NotificationDeliveryJob.java`** -- `@Component` with two `@Scheduled(fixedRate = 60_000)` methods:
  - `processScheduledNotifications` -- finds PENDING with scheduledAt <= now, marks SENT, updates receipt deliveredAt
  - `retryFailedNotifications` -- finds FAILED with nextRetryAt <= now and retryCount < maxRetries, attempts re-delivery with exponential backoff from `AppConfig.notification.retryDelaySeconds`

### Controller (1 file)
- **`controller/NotificationController.java`** -- `@RestController` at `/api/v1/notifications` with 10 endpoints:
  - `GET /` -- paginated list with unread count
  - `GET /{id}` -- detail with object-level auth
  - `POST /send` -- ADMINISTRATOR only
  - `POST /announce` -- ADMINISTRATOR only, bulk
  - `PUT /{id}/read` -- mark read
  - `PUT /read-all` -- mark all read
  - `PUT /{id}/cancel` -- cancel scheduled
  - `GET /unread-count` -- unread count
  - `GET /templates` -- ADMINISTRATOR only
  - `POST /templates` -- ADMINISTRATOR only

All files follow existing codebase conventions: Lombok (`@Getter/@Setter/@Builder/@RequiredArgsConstructor/@Slf4j`), `@Transactional`, `AuthContext` for auth, `AuditService` for audit logging, `AppConfig` for retry settings, `AppException` for error handling, and `@Valid` for request validation. The `@EnableScheduling` annotation is already present on `CampusFitApplication`.</result>
